title: W2: olean codec read — three validation levels over the extracted contract
description:
Implement the olean codec, read side (fln-olean, §7.2): header parsing (magic, version fields, base address), region relocation via the shared compacted-region machinery, ModuleData traversal into Grimoire environment structures, and the three validation levels — trust-the-producer (mmap speed, the Reference posture), structural audit (tag/bounds/reachability sweep, still fast), full recheck (Crucible over every constant — the G1 mode).

The layout inventory comes from OLEAN_CONTRACT.md (W1 extraction) with per-field provenance and a versioning note per Reference release in the supported epoch set. Hostile inputs are a first-class threat (R18): every decode path runs under resource budgets; a malformed olean yields typed ArtifactCorrupt / ArtifactEpochMismatch, never a panic and never unbounded work.

Acceptance: the full mathlib olean set at the pin loads at every validation level; read throughput meets the PG-8 floor (>= Reference load throughput) on the baseline profile; epoch-mismatch inputs produce the typed error with the correct epoch fields. Tests: the C3 artifact-archaeology corpus (valid + mutated oleans, every offset and tag targeted); decode-budget exhaustion fixtures; differential load (same olean into Reference and FrankenLean, environment statement-diff empty).

title: W2: Effects on asupersync — IO/Task semantics, lab-virtualizable, capability-observed
description:
Implement effects on asupersync (§6.7): IO, timers, filesystem, process, and task pools behind the Reference IO/Task semantics — Task.spawn/bind/get observable ordering, IO.Ref atomicity, IO.Process behavior, the interruption/checkSystem polling contract — displacing the vendored-libuv event loop wholesale.

Under the lab runtime the ENTIRE effect plane virtualizes (virtual time, injected faults, seeded schedules): this is how the Tribunal replays server sessions and torture-tests cancellation, and it is why no detached thread or raw OS handle may bypass the Cx discipline anywhere in the workspace. In faithful mode default ambient authority approximates an ordinary Lean process (drop-in means unchanged packages just run, with effects OBSERVED into provenance); sound mode can require declared capabilities and fail closed; the --pure-elab audit mode (W5) builds on this seam.

Acceptance: the G0-3 IO/Task parity micro-corpus passes on the production effect plane, then again under the lab runtime with virtual time (same observable results — the virtualization is invisible to compliant code); cancellation drains regions deterministically (obligation-leak oracle clean). Tests: Task ordering fixtures incl. nested spawn/bind chains; IO.Ref atomicity stress under lab schedules; checkSystem interruption-latency fixtures; capability-observation events verified in the provenance journal.

title: W2: Term store — total interning, alpha-canonical digest, expanded-weight budgets
description:
Implement the term store with total contractual interning (§7.1b): expressions, levels, and names hash-consed contractually, not opportunistically — one node per full structural identity INCLUDING binder names and mdata (metaprograms observe them; faithful Expr behavior demands exact round-trip), with pointer/ID equality BEING structural equality: Expr.equal collapses to a compare; kernel caches key on identity pairs.

Plus: an auxiliary alpha-canonical digest (binder names stripped) indexing kernel-side defeq/whnf caches so alpha-varied duplicates share reduction work without confusing the metaprogram-visible plane. Sharing is maximal and MEASURED: every store keeps sharing statistics and enforces expanded-weight budgets — a term small on the wire can be astronomical unshared; an adversarial DAG-bomb is a first-class typed inspectable condition (§8.2c counts expanded weight, not bytes). Serialization preserves sharing exactly in both codecs.

Acceptance: interning invariant (structural equal implies pointer equal) holds under property tests across construction paths; alpha-digest cache sharing demonstrated on the G0-7 defeq fixtures; DAG-bomb fixtures produce typed Inconclusive under budget, never OOM or hang. Tests: sharing-statistics assertions on Corpus-derived fixtures; round-trip sharing preservation through both codecs; concurrent interning determinism at {1,8,32} threads.

title: W8: Lake surface — lakefile.lean on Golem, dual rebuild decisions, transactional resolution
description:
Implement the Lake surface (fln-lake, §13.3): lake CLI-compatible — lakefile.lean EXECUTES on Golem (the configuration DSL is just Lean; user code, so Golem executing it IS the compatibility mechanism), lakefile.toml takes the fast path, targets/facets map onto Ledger queries, require fetches via the D2 git tool through the shared subprocess capsule, lean-toolchain and elan layout honored so elan multiplexes FrankenLean toolchains transparently. Exit codes, --json, and the query surface match the pin.

The behavior upgrade is sound-mode and Behavior-Noted (BN-03): decl-granular no-op detection, with --faithful-invalidation restoring file-cone behavior for byte-for-byte CI reproduction studies. Internally TWO rebuild decisions are computed every time: the Reference decision (what the pin Lake would rebuild — faithful mode never omits an output upstream would produce, though it may satisfy it from a valid Ledger record and still project expected files/traces) and the native decision (which demand nodes actually changed); fln build explain <target> shows both, the delta, changed input identities, opaque barriers, and cache outcomes. Dependency resolution is transactional (a failed fetch never leaves a half-rewritten manifest presented as complete) and emits a resolution receipt; hermeticity is reported on the H0-H4 ladder, never overclaimed.

This bead also OWNS open question OQ-9: whether a native git-protocol client eventually replaces the D2 git tool, or git stays a permanent optional external — resolved here with the decision recorded in the plan decision log (the default posture is permanent D2 status, matching upstream Lake own contract; a native client is only justified if the closed-universe benefit outweighs the protocol-maintenance cost).

Acceptance: real Lake projects (incl. mathlib layout) configure and build unmodified; the Lake transcript corpus (C6) diffs clean on CLI surfaces; fln build explain output validated on fixture edits; OQ-9 decision recorded. Tests: lakefile.lean execution differentials; interrupted-fetch drills (C6 interrupted updates); manifest-atomicity fault drills; exit-code/JSON transcript fixtures.

title: W5: Golem — FIR pipeline, FLBC interpreter, intrinsic table
description:
Workstream W5 "Golem" (§11): the metaprogram compiler and VM — the vehicle for ALL user-class Lean code (mathlib tactics, project macros, lakefiles, #eval, plugins) under the Oracle-Only Law (upstream implementation never rides it; the only Lean it executes is user code and source-elaborated library code per §4.3).

Pipeline: elaborated terms -> FIR (ANF/LCNF-class IR keeping the Reference-established disciplines: specialization, simplification, closure conversion, explicit boxing, RC insertion with borrow inference + constructor-reuse/FBIP — performance-observable, the ecosystem tuned against it) -> FLBC register bytecode whose values ARE Marrow ABI objects (the quiet masterstroke: zero conversion across interpreted code, Iron-compiled code, Reference-built plugins, and the Mirror facade — one calling convention for the whole membrane). Every pass is a pure ValidatedIr -> (ValidatedIr, PassWitness) transformation with validators; the RC-insertion ownership validator is simpler than the optimizer it audits. Interpreter: threaded dispatch, inline caches, allocation through Marrow so heartbeats count identically, Reference semantics for panics/partial/stack/checkSystem (PG-7 floor: >= 3x Reference interpreter on the tactic micro-corpus). Intrinsic table generated from the @[extern] census (Appendix C). Initialization ordering + capability-scoped effects incl. --pure-elab audit mode (§11.5). native_decide staged repair Tier 1: replay receipts (§11.6).

Exit: gate G2 "The Golem Wakes" — user-class code runs with #eval/IO/Task parity, Reference-built plugins load and run, intrinsic census fully populated with fixtures.

## Success Criteria

All scope and outcomes stated above remain mandatory, and every dependency/child deliverable required for this epic or gate must be completed at its honest evidence level. The MANDATORY EPIC/GATE CLOSEOUT CONTRACT in Notes must pass with coverage mapping, no-mock E2E evidence, detailed archived human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, reproducible environment facts, and every skip/divergence classified. No feature, compatibility surface, invariant, performance gate, or release claim may be silently narrowed, dropped, or replaced by a prototype.
design: REVIEW AMENDMENT — the workstream exit includes the explicit no-mock integration bead franken_lean-golem-runtime-integration-pbe. Individual compiler, interpreter, intrinsic, effect, and plugin-loader beads may close on their stable component contracts; the W5 epic and G2 evidence cannot close until the real user-code stack is exercised end to end with no production test stubs.
notes:
MANDATORY EPIC/GATE CLOSEOUT CONTRACT:
This epic/gate may close only when every child deliverable is complete; the verification registry maps every implemented public behavior, FL-INV claim, Parity-Ledger row, Behavior Note, and PG/gate claim to named unit/property/mutation coverage plus a shared no-mock E2E scenario; detailed human + schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts are archived; all divergences and skips are classified; no mock-only evidence is used for a release claim; and the final evidence bundle records exact reproduction commands, environment/epoch/mode/platform/thread/seed/cache facts, input hashes, receipts/logical roots, expected final states, first divergences, and cleanup results.

title: W1: Baseline performance report — measure the Reference before ratifying gates
description:
Produce the W1 baseline performance report (§19.1): measure the Reference at the pin on the two pinned bare-metal profiles (32-core x86-64 Linux; Apple-silicon macOS) BEFORE any gate number is finalized. Every §19.2 gate ratio is ratified or amended against this report — a gate number without a measured baseline is marketing.

Measure: full-Corpus build wall (cold and warm-cache); per-file elaboration time distribution (p50/p95/p99); kernel-recheck time of the olean set; server worker import/attach time and RSS per worker on representative mathlib files; first-goal latency distribution; the interpreter micro-corpus; the bv_decide corpus with CaDiCaL. Record host facts, isolation checks, cache states, sample counts, and RAW samples (runs failing host-quality checks are retained as invalid, never averaged in — §19.2 discipline).

Acceptance: the report is a checked-in, regenerable artifact (script + data + rendered summary) with every number carrying corpus, machine, cache state, and dispersion; the PG-1..PG-M table is re-ratified against it and any amendment is recorded in the plan decision log. Tests: the measurement scripts are deterministic in methodology (seeded ordering, pinned thread counts) and re-runnable by one command; a second run on the same host must reproduce medians within the declared variance budget.

title: W2: D2 subprocess capsule — the one sanctioned tool-spawn path, fully provenanced
description:
Implement the D2 subprocess protocol as one shared capsule (Rule D2): the single implementation through which the ONLY two external tools FrankenLean may ever spawn — the system C compiler (only from the --backend c code generator, mirroring upstream leanc) and system git (only from Lake-compatible dependency fetching, mirroring upstream Lake) — are invoked. Nothing else in the program spawns processes, ever (no cc at check time, no CaDiCaL, no curl, no tar, no LaTeX), and CI enforces that this capsule is the only process-spawn site outside the Tribunal sandbox.

The protocol, in full: argv-only invocation (no shell interpretation, ever); per-job private temp directories; timeouts with process-TREE cancellation (no orphaned grandchildren); output-size limits; environment allowlist (nothing ambient leaks in); atomic result publication (a killed job leaves staging, never a half-written product); and the executable path AND CONTENT HASH recorded into provenance so a build record names exactly which cc/git produced what. Absence of a tool yields a typed CapabilityDenied error NAMING the alternative (Iron for cc; atp/HTTPS CAS sync guidance for git) — never silent substitution. Products of both tools are excluded from --reproducible artifact certification by construction (D17), and the Reference toolchain is NOT a third tool: a release check proves no fln binary can locate, spawn, or link it (D8).

Acceptance: leanc --backend c and Lake require both route through the capsule with full provenance rows; the spawn-site CI scan finds exactly one call site; kill/timeout drills leave no orphan processes and no partial products; absent-tool fixtures produce the typed error with the named alternative. Tests: process-tree cancellation drills (grandchild spawned by cc must die with the job); env-allowlist tests (a canary env var must not reach the child); output-bomb tests (limit enforced, typed failure, no OOM); provenance-hash tests (swapping the cc binary changes the recorded content hash and invalidates dependent D4-class records).

title: W7: norm_num + omega — native certificate-producing arithmetic cores
description:
Implement norm_num/omega native cores (§12.4): norm_num-class evaluation and the omega decision procedure with native cores (fln-bignum-backed), certificate-producing, kernel-replayed — every result enters the environment only through a kernel-checked artifact (FL-INV-06; the engines are untrusted by constitution, which is what makes native reimplementation safe).

Scope: numeric normalization over the Reference-visible semiring/ring/field structure conventions; omega over linear integer arithmetic with the upstream-observable goal coverage; certificates in the §8.6 discipline (simpler-than-recomputation checkers, recompute fallback); facade rows for the tactic entry points with golden-trace fixtures from the instrumented oracle.

Acceptance: agreement with the Reference on the norm_num/omega corpus slices (same goals closed, same goals refused); certificates replay through Crucible; performance within the envelope the PG-2 model assigns to these lanes. Tests: differential goal-closure fixtures; certificate round-trip + tamper tests; property tests over generated arithmetic goals (agreement with a big-integer reference evaluator); budget-exhaustion yields typed Inconclusive per FL-INV-07.

title: W4: Quill engine — Pratt parser, lossless green trees, exact SourceInfo
description:
Implement the Quill engine (fln-parse, fln-syntax; §9.1): token tables + Pratt-precedence parsing with the Reference category/leading/trailing model; Syntax trees with identical node kinds, atom/ident/node structure, and SourceInfo discipline (original vs synthetic positions, leading/trailing whitespace attachment, UTF-8 byte positions exactly as upstream computes them — wire-visible, therefore conformance surface); the full extensibility contract: parsing INTERLEAVES with elaboration (a command can install parsers used three lines later), commands install syntax/macro/elab rules visible immediately, precedence interactions match.

Internals (ours to design): lossless rope + persistent green tree (exact bytes, trivia, comments, UTF-8/UTF-16 positions) with red views adding context pointers per document snapshot; every node carries an origin set (literal span, macro-definition span, macro-call span, quotation spans, recovered markers) projected to the Reference single-primary-span convention at the wire while the full provenance graph feeds Palimpsest.

Acceptance: G0-4 curated corpus trees/positions byte-agree; C1/C2 parse products diff clean against the Reference frontend under instrumentation; the interleaving contract demonstrated (fixture where a command installs syntax used later in the same file). Tests: token-table and precedence property tests; UTF-8/UTF-16 position conversion fixtures (LSP boundary); green-tree losslessness (parse then print = identical bytes) across the corpus.

1. Unit coverage: deterministic happy-path, boundary, malformed-input, resource-exhaustion, cancellation, and failure-atomicity cases for every new public behavior; property/metamorphic and mutation-kill tests wherever an invariant or equivalence is claimed.
2. E2E coverage: add or extend at least one shared scripts/e2e scenario for the real user-visible path, plus a negative/recovery scenario. Release-level evidence uses the no-mock lane (real Reference binary/artifact/filesystem/editor/process/host as applicable). A mock may support a unit test but may not close an L-level, PG gate, or public claim.
3. Detailed logs: emit both concise human logs and schema-versioned NDJSON with run/scenario/step ids; bead, claim, invariant, Parity-Ledger row, and gate ids; exact command/argv with secrets redacted; cwd; epoch/mode/profile/platform/thread-count/seed/cache-state/host facts; canonical input hashes; expected vs actual; monotonic start/end/duration; resource and budget facts; exit status/signal; stdout/stderr artifact references; receipt/logical roots; first-divergence data; cleanup and asserted final state. Never swallow stderr, never leak secrets, and bound adversarial log volume.
4. CI/closeout: archive logs, fixtures, repro bundles, and evidence manifests; failures must be diagnosable from artifacts alone. Run scripts/check.sh, cargo test, the bead-specific unit/property suites, relevant Tribunal differential/mutation/fault lanes, and the shared E2E scenario. The close evidence names every test/scenario id and artifact location; skips require a typed, logged, honest L-level limitation and may not satisfy the skipped claim.

title: W12: Distribution — static binaries, elan layout, reproducible dual-builds, D8 release audit
description:
Implement distribution (§17.1, §22.2 W12): static binaries per platform (lean, leanc, lake, fln) with the elan-compatible release layout so a lean-toolchain line names a FrankenLean toolchain and elan multiplexes it transparently; the install script; side-by-side epoch installs; the version-line law — parse-compatible with the Reference (version/commit fields preserved) plus a separate machine-readable implementation field; fln identity --json (implementation commit, epoch, profile, lockfile, TCB hash, evidence freshness); an exact-reference transcript mode for conformance harnesses only. Compatibility never becomes deception (§17.1).

Reproducible releases: release binaries built TWICE in isolated builders and byte-compared (divergence blocks); --reproducible certified across the matrix (linux-x86-64, linux-aarch64, macos-aarch64; windows bit-certification a separate declared decision, D17); the stdlib fixpoint (elaborating Init/Std from clean state twice — and once more through a freshly rebuilt toolchain — produces bit-identical oleans and logical roots: the native-world descendant of the classic triple bootstrap). The D8 release check: CI proves release binaries contain no symbol, path, or capability by which the Reference could be located, spawned, or linked; the ORACLE_FALLBACK harness is compiled out and proven absent.

Acceptance: cold-machine install (C9: clean-machine, air-gapped, path/locale variation) yields a working toolchain that builds a real project; dual-build byte-identity green; elan integration verified against real elan. Tests: install-script matrix; C9 bootstrap/reproducibility corpus; release-content audit (symbol scan, capability scan); toolchain-switching fixtures via lean-toolchain files.

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.

title: W1: Workspace scaffolding — §21 crate map, pinned nightly, unsafe-ledger CI, layering checks
description:
Scaffold the cargo workspace per the cycle-free crate map (§21): every fln-* crate stubbed with its one-line charter as crate-level docs — fln-core, fln-hash, fln-bignum, fln-libm, fln-rt, fln-unsafe-abi, fln-unsafe-region, fln-env, fln-olean, fln-kernel, fln-checker, fln-parse, fln-syntax, fln-elab, fln-comp, fln-vm, fln-unsafe-jit (feature iron), fln-anvil, fln-verdict, fln-ledger, fln-lake, fln-server, fln-trace, fln-hound, fln-doc, fln-mcp, fln-tui, fln-cli, fln (library facade), fln-conformance, fln-wasm — plus vendor/lean4-src at the pin (three D8 roles only, Apache-2.0 notices carried, never linked).

Structural enforcement from day one: rust-toolchain.toml pinning the exact nightly (no or-later); #![forbid(unsafe_code)] at every ordinary crate root; the three fln-unsafe-* boundary crates with #![deny(unsafe_code)] roots and the unsafe-boundary ledger file (path, invariant, evidence, safe fallback, no-claim boundary per row); CI checks that (a) walk the dependency graph and fail on any edge violating the strictly-downward §5 layering or the D3 structural laws (no fln-unsafe-* crate depends on fln-kernel/fln-checker; no unsafe crate exports a type launderable into a checked declaration), (b) reject any unledgered unsafe site, (c) enforce the fln-kernel line-count covenant once the crate has content.

Acceptance: cargo check --all-targets green on the stubbed workspace from a cold checkout; the layering CI check demonstrably fails on a seeded illegal edge (add a test-only violation in CI to prove detection, then remove); the unsafe-ledger check fails on a seeded unledgered allow site. Tests: the CI checks themselves plus a workspace-graph snapshot test that any new crate or edge must be explicitly acknowledged in a reviewed file.

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.
design:
REVIEW AMENDMENT — close repository-configuration and bootstrap gaps in the structural contract. Until an explicit reviewed Cargo-config contract exists, both `.cargo/config.toml` and legacy `.cargo/config` are forbidden because they can inject lint caps, compiler wrappers, linkers/runners, and source replacement outside Cargo.toml/WORKSPACE_GRAPH. The exact nightly must be validated structurally as one `[toolchain]` table with one dated channel and recognized typed keys only; path, decoy-section, duplicate, and unknown-key forms fail closed. CI performs the same SUITE.lock↔rust-toolchain validation with system Python before any repository-selected rustc executes, installs the explicit lock channel, and checks rustc commit via `rustup run <channel> rustc -Vv`. Seed both Cargo-config filenames and malformed/ambiguous toolchain shapes; retain the original layering, unsafe-ledger, closure, line-covenant, and no-mock evidence requirements unchanged.

REVIEW AMENDMENT — seal ambient compiler configuration. The structural lint posture is not sufficient by itself: the pinned rustc accepts unsafe code under both crate-level deny(unsafe_code) and forbid(unsafe_code) when `--cap-lints allow` is injected. Every authoritative gate must therefore invoke the exact locked cargo/rustc explicitly and fail closed or sanitize all ambient compiler/configuration channels, including RUSTFLAGS, CARGO_ENCODED_RUSTFLAGS, rustc wrapper overrides, RUSTUP_TOOLCHAIN, Cargo home/ancestor config, target-specific rustflags, and any equivalent channel discovered by the implementation. The seal must not destroy user state or shared caches: use isolated per-run homes/targets or validated read-only inputs. Log the effective compiler identity and admitted environment in the evidence envelope. Keep source-level unsafe-ledger scanning as an independent defense; environment sealing does not replace it.
acceptance_criteria: All original description requirements and the mandatory verification definition of done remain binding. In addition, the hostile-environment mutation lane must prove the gate cannot be made to accept an unledgered unsafe operation by injecting `--cap-lints allow`, a fake rustc/workspace wrapper, an alternate RUSTUP_TOOLCHAIN, target-specific rustflags, or Cargo config from the repository, an ancestor directory, or Cargo home. Each case must produce a typed pre-build rejection or demonstrate that the channel was excluded, followed by a clean recovery pass. Unit tests also reject duplicate/repeated root workspace and package/dependency keys or sections so the constrained manifest parser fails closed on ambiguous TOML instead of silently choosing a last value.

comments.author: RubyForest
comments.text: Pre-commit review of the active manifest.rs hardening draft: (1) unquote strips only the outer quote bytes and rejects backslashes, but does not reject an interior quote, so malformed TOML-like values such as a feature array entry containing adjacent quoted strings can be accepted as one string. Seed a malformed interior-quote/adjacent-string case and make unquote require no interior quote bytes. (2) dependency_path skips every empty split_table_fields field, so leading or doubled commas in an inline dependency table are accepted; only one final trailing comma should be allowed, matching string_array. Seed leading-comma and doubled-comma inline-table cases. Also reject an empty path string explicitly. These are parser-shape findings only; I did not edit the reserved/active file.

comments.author: RubyForest
comments.text: Independent post-commit verification for 882bdb4: rch exec -- cargo test -p structure-guard completed exit 0 with 83 passed / 0 failed across unit, closure, real-workspace, and seeded suites. This confirms the committed suite is green; comment 94 identifies two adversarial parser cases not represented by those 83 tests and therefore still needing coverage/fix.

comments.author: RubyForest
comments.text: Adjacent structural-parser review: graph.rs remains permissive where manifest.rs is now fail-closed. A crate row can repeat rank= or kind= and silently take the last value; allow-direct silently drops leading/doubled empty entries and permits duplicate deps; duplicate edges, prohibits, and suite-dep rows are accepted. Since WORKSPACE_GRAPH is the reviewed structural contract, these ambiguous duplicate/empty shapes should fail with seeded regressions (plus name/pattern validation where currently absent), not normalize silently.

comments.author: RubyForest
comments.text: Bootstrap-adjacent review: the pre-rustc workflow invokes plain python3 while importing tomllib. Python normally places the repository working directory on sys.path, so a checked-in root tomllib.py/package (or ambient PYTHONPATH) can shadow the stdlib parser before structural Rust checks run. Use isolated mode (python3 -I, plus an explicit minimal environment as appropriate) for every trusted bootstrap snippet and seed a shadow-module/hostile-PYTHONPATH rejection case. This belongs with the newly added ambient compiler-environment acceptance.

title: W2: Owned allocator — size-classed, heartbeat hook, membrane discipline
description:
Implement the owned allocator (§6.3): size-classed, mimalloc-tradition (thread-local pages, size-class bins, deferred frees) — owned rather than off-the-shelf because allocation behavior is SEMANTICALLY visible through heartbeat accounting (fuel parity, §4.2/§8.5) and performance-visible everywhere.

The heartbeat hook lives here: one counting law, calibrated by spike G0-6, shared by both kernel engines and Golem (allocation through Marrow so heartbeats count identically across interpreted and native paths). The allocator must also serve the CompatHeap discipline (§6.1b): membrane-only allocation and free, so foreign code observes exactly the Reference allocation contract at the ABI.

Acceptance: fuel-parity fixtures from G0-6 stay green when running over the real allocator; allocation-heavy corpus benchmarks show no pathological regression vs the baseline report envelope; zero leaks over the PG-6 four-hour soak profile (measured under the lab runtime with the leak oracle). Tests: size-class boundary unit tests; concurrent alloc/free stress under the asupersync lab with seed replay; heartbeat-count determinism test at thread counts {1, 8, 32} (same input, same tick totals — FL-INV-01 applies to fuel too).

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.

title: W1: Performance-gate harness — bench binaries, baselines, tail gates, flame artifacts
description:
Build the performance-gate harness (§19.2, §19.1 discipline): the infrastructure every PG gate (PG-1 the Judge, PG-2 cold corpus, PG-3 the cone, PG-4 attach, PG-5 determinism, PG-6 memory, PG-7 Golem, PG-8 codec, PG-9 Verdict, PG-K bignum, PG-10 cache, PG-L latency, PG-1b consensus tax, PG-M the Mirror) plugs into — one harness, not fourteen ad-hoc scripts.

Per gate: a bench binary; a committed, versioned baseline; a variance budget; alert-first thresholds graduating to blocking after two releases of baseline (the manim discipline); regression gating on TAILS as well as medians (interactive p95 > 5% blocks; kernel-throughput > 5% blocks; build wall/RSS > 7% blocks); a flamegraph artifact automatically attached on regression identifying the bottleneck; a rollback path recorded for risky changes. Every benchmark bundle records host facts, isolation checks, cache state, sample counts, and RAW samples — runs failing host-quality checks are retained as invalid, never averaged in. Scaling profiles (parallel efficiency at {1,8,32,96} threads for elaboration-bound and check-bound workloads) publish per release. Every published number carries its §20.4 claim state: OBSERVED on a named corpus and machine, or TARGETED — documentation CI (W12 Witness) enforces that the README can never say "2x faster than Lean" off one warm-cache loop.

Detailed logging is constitutive: each bench run emits structured NDJSON (per-phase timings, host fingerprint, cache state, sample-by-sample results) archived as CI artifacts so any regression is diagnosable from artifacts alone. Acceptance: the harness runs the first real gate end-to-end (PG-8 or PG-K are the earliest candidates) against the W1 baseline with the full bundle emitted; a seeded regression trips the tail gate and attaches the flame artifact. Tests: harness self-test with planted regressions per threshold class; baseline-drift detection (a silently edited baseline fails CI); host-quality-check rejection fixtures; bundle-schema contract tests.

title: W2: Grimoire environment — persistent maps, O(1) snapshots, extension registry, logical roots
description:
Implement the Grimoire environment (fln-env, §7.1): semantically the Reference environment (name -> constant map over axioms/defs/theorems/opaques/inductives/ctors/recursors/quotients; environment extensions with import-time replay preserving upstream ordering semantics exactly; attribute state; import graph), mechanically ours: persistent HAMT-style maps with structural sharing so snapshots are O(1) — the primitive under Athanor speculative parallelism (§10.6), Lantern per-request views (§14.3), and Envoy search trees (§16.3).

Key contracts: mathlib custom extensions round-trip as region data WITHOUT executing code; an extension payload understood only as opaque bytes is preserved losslessly, flagged in provenance, and honestly blocks fine-grained invalidation through it (never guessed safe); every extension declares merge + checkpoint semantics in a registry (branch/merge includes extensions BY CONTRACT); each commit carries a logical root (digest excluding wall-clock/paths/schedule — the cache key the Ledger, receipts, and Envoy all speak) plus a separate operational-metadata root.

Acceptance: environments decoded from real mathlib oleans (G0-1) materialize with extension replay matching Reference-observable order; snapshot fork + read costs O(1) verified; logical roots stable across hosts for identical inputs. Tests: extension-ordering differential fixtures; snapshot isolation property tests (mutation after fork invisible to the fork); logical-root determinism across thread counts and two machines.

title: W3: Certificates, receipts, transparency log — auditable checking
description:
Implement certificates, receipts, and the transparency log (§8.6): every checked declaration can emit a certificate — the compressed, structure-shared elaborated term DAG plus reduction hints that make independent rechecking cheap (unfold order, literal-arithmetic transcripts so a tiny verifier replays accelerated ops without its own bignum trust). Certificates attach to olean-next records, feed fln export (speaking the community established export format for cross-prover checkers), and may ship a warm defeq-cache cartridge (memoized reduction history — third-party rechecking becomes verification rather than search).

The certificate law (D20): the checker must be SIMPLER than recomputation, must reject unknown versions, and must fall back to recomputation on any failure — an accelerator, never a wider TCB. Receipts bind kernel + checker build hashes, the consensus policy, fuel profiles, and both verdicts. Attested receipts append to a transparency log (Merkle Mountain Range over receipt roots, signed checkpoints): third parties verify inclusion and consistency without trusting the logger; equivocation is detectable — "checked" becomes an artifact anyone can audit, not a verb someone witnessed.

Acceptance: certificate emit + independent replay on the corpus with the checker measurably simpler (line count, dependency count) than the engines; fln export output accepted by at least one external checker; MMR inclusion/consistency proofs verified by an independent implementation of the verifier side; PG-1b receipt-emission overhead <= 1 ms/decl amortized. Tests: certificate-version rejection fixtures; corrupt-certificate fallback-to-recompute fixtures; log equivocation detection test (forked checkpoint must be caught).

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. Update the decision log and add compatibility/tamper/resource fixtures for both formats.

title: W6: Synod instance engine — exact semantics, SearchCards, keyed negative caches
description:
Implement Synod, the instance engine (§10.4): the Reference semantics — priorities, outParam/semiOutParam, default instances, tabling/caching behavior, the results the ecosystem diamond structures depend on — behind a rebuilt engine: instance search compiled to an indexed relational form (discrimination-automaton candidate retrieval, subsumption-checked tabling, cycle detection with franken_networkx diagnostics), with provenance recorded per resolution: every attempted instance, why it failed (unification residue, priority loss, depth), as a Palimpsest tree.

Every search emits a SearchCard (explored candidates, priorities, unification residues, cycle decisions, cache hits, selection) — provenance and negative-cache key in one artifact, with negative entries valid ONLY under their exact dependency key (instance-table identity, transparency, options, local-context fingerprint). Faithful mode pins search order to the Reference; sound mode may reorder only within proven-confluent regions (same result, better time), CGSE-registered. Name resolution, coercion graphs, and discrimination indexes are immutable content-addressed derivations of the environment commit, incrementally maintained and Ledger-cached — faithful iteration order preserved even where internals are faster.

Acceptance: instance-search outcomes match the oracle across the Corpus instance-trace corpus (instrumented-oracle instance traces); SearchCards complete per perturbation; the sound-mode "why is there no instance" answer (§15.3) renders from this engine exhaust with no second implementation. Tests: golden instance-trace replay; diamond/priority fixtures; negative-cache validity mutation tests (a stale negative entry must be impossible under the dependency key); cycle-diagnostic fixtures.

title: W1: Tribunal bootstrap — epoch lab, differential harness, Parity Ledger schema
description:
Bootstrap the Tribunal (fln-conformance, §18) FIRST — before the subsystems it will judge — so every workstream is born differential. The Reference runs INSIDE the harness as the standing differential oracle, forever (Oracle-Only Law capacity 1).

Scope: the epoch laboratory (official release archives with hashes; executable/symbol/header inventories; CLI and LSP transcripts; artifact fixtures; quirk/defect ledgers; environment-normalization recipes; exact reproduction commands; per platform; immutable once published — discoveries create reviewed hashed revisions); the oracle-precedence ladder implemented as data (core theory > observed binary behavior > sources/tests > stable downstream dependence > documented divergence > unclassified-blocks); comparison classes as versioned normalizer code with fixtures (a normalizer may strip only declared-nonsemantic fields and can never discard an error body to pass); the corpus family scaffolding C0-C9; upstream test-suite import at the pin with expected-outcome mapping; the Parity Ledger schema (row-per-symbol: surface, symbol, kind, semantic status, L-level, mode, oracle kind, comparison class, fixtures, determinism class, claim state, freshness) with the regeneration + regression CI job; the ORACLE_FALLBACK poison-tag machinery and the release compile-out check (§18.10, D8).

Acceptance: a Reference-vs-Reference smoke differential runs green end-to-end (proves harness plumbing); the Parity Ledger generates and the dashboard aggregates; C1 corpus imported. Tests: the harness tests itself via seeded-divergence fixtures (a planted diff must be detected and triaged, never normalized away).

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. design: REVIEW AMENDMENT — the Tribunal remains the first substantive subsystem, but it starts only after the authoritative SUITE.lock/closure audit and shared verification/logging contract exist. This avoids an unpinned oracle lab and a parallel ad-hoc test runner while still allowing contract extraction, fln-core, fln-hash, and platform work to proceed in parallel after workspace scaffolding.

comments.author: RubyForest
comments.text: Early read-only acceptance review of the active bootstrap: (1) ledger::validate_fixtures does root.join(fixture).exists() without rejecting absolute paths, .. traversal, symlink escape, or non-regular targets, so a purported repo-relative evidence row can cite arbitrary host files. Enforce lexical and canonical confinement to the workspace and retain source line numbers for exact diagnostics. (2) Normalizer::paths_v1 accepts arbitrary strings and globally replaces them. A caller can pass error, x, or an entire diagnostic body; the three substring markers only catch some deletions and one surviving marker can mask another discarded diagnostic. Validate absolute path prefixes and normalize structured path fields/boundaries, with multiple-error/warning/unsolved-goal mutation tests. (3) OracleFallback::use_poisoned_diagnosis_value_gate_inert returns raw T, structurally laundering away the poison tag; a method name cannot enforce gate/cache inertness. Keep the taint in the return type or expose diagnostic-only operations that cannot yield an unpoisoned product. Also, the current L0-no-fixture test comment expects acceptance but asserts parse error; cargo test should catch that transient inconsistency.

comments.author: RubyForest
comments.text: Second acceptance pass on the newly added ledger/tests: the real ledger currently promotes p8a observables to L2 OBSERVED even though p8a is open and its cited core_observables lane has known unbounded-Nat/Int, corruption-validation, and corpus-totality gaps (p8a comment 9). Plan 4.2 defines L2 as a gated corpus pass with exclusions explicit; either keep these rows at honest L0/L1/TARGETED or record explicit exclusions and only advance them when the owning evidence closes. The String.hash row also cites BLAKE3 vectors, which are fln-hash identity evidence rather than evidence for Lean String.hash. Separately, poison_compile_out::the_scanner_detects_a_planted_leak only calls str.contains on a literal; it does not exercise collect_rs_files or the real violation scanner, so a broken traversal survives the planted test. Factor the scanner and seed a real isolated source tree. The cfg!(feature) default test proves only the test build omitted the feature, not yet the promised release-artifact absence check.

comments.author: RubyForest
comments.text: Epoch-lab/E2E acceptance review: gen_epoch_manifest.sh generate mode mkdir -p plus redirects directly into an existing published epoch, so it can overwrite an allegedly immutable lab and leave stale transcript files when the slice changes. Generate in a new scratch snapshot, validate completeness, then require an explicit reviewed first publication; existing epochs should be check-only. Set LC_ALL/TZ for the whole generator so glob/tool inventory ordering is not inherited-locale dependent. The comment claims a self-contained slice, but select_slice only chooses the ten smallest files and performs no self-containment classification. More importantly, ref_vs_ref.sh invents a minimal fln-e2e/1 logger instead of extending the shared fln.e2e/2 evidence family: early failures have no terminal record or committed manifest/bundle; JSON strings are printf-unescaped; there are no canonical input roots, epoch/mode/profile/thread/seed/cache facts, per-step supervisor/resource/exit artifacts, bounded output, cancellation/recovery, or failure-atomic final-state proof. Under rur and this bead mandatory DoD, a passing diff alone cannot close the E2E claim; route the oracle commands through the shared supervisor/evidence contract and validate/commit the bundle.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Bootstrap implemented + verified by session agent (claim still blocked by rur/xwf). Delivered: Parity Ledger schema + seeded ledger + gate test; comparison-class normalizers with never-discard-an-error law + planted-divergence tests; oracle-precedence ladder as data; ORACLE_FALLBACK poison machinery behind oracle-fallback-dev with compile-out test + workspace leak grep; epoch lab tribunal/epochs/v4.32.0 (verified binary identity, executable inventory, env-normalization recipe, C1 slice of 10 upstream elab tests with recorded baselines, drift mode); ref_vs_ref.sh smoke differential green end-to-end (twice-run byte-identity, baseline match, planted divergence detected, recovery). Commit 06ba84b. Scope notes (typed): C1 import uses plain-invocation transcripts as observed-behavior baseline (upstream-harness semantics + full C0-C9 scaffolding + instrumented oracle are follow-on beads); dashboard aggregation exists as the ledger aggregate API + gate test, visual dashboard pending. Will close when claimable.

comments.author: RubyForest
comments.text: Post-commit acceptance audit of 06ba84b (bead correctly remains open): (1) check.sh only adds gen_epoch_manifest.sh/ref_vs_ref.sh to governed inputs and shellcheck; neither `gen_epoch_manifest.sh --check` nor `ref_vs_ref.sh` is executed by scripts/check.sh or .github/workflows/ci.yml, so the required regeneration/regression CI job and permanent Reference-vs-Reference obligation do not exist. (2) Ledger rows store oracle_kind and comparison_class as unchecked strings, disconnected from OracleAuthority/ComparisonClass/NormalizerId; typos or invented authorities/normalizers pass the gate. Make these parsed typed/versioned values and reject unknown entries. (3) The committed epoch lab has executable hashes and 10 plain-invocation transcripts, but the bead also mandates symbol/header inventories, CLI and LSP transcripts, artifact fixtures, explicit quirk/defect ledgers, per-platform labs, C0-C9 scaffolding, and upstream expected-outcome mapping. The comment classifying those as follow-on cannot close this bead because its acceptance explicitly forbids deferring or narrowing listed scope. Earlier comments 27-29/33 also remain unresolved (fixture escape, arbitrary normalizer prefixes, poison laundering, overclaimed L2 rows, fake scanner mutation, mutable published epoch, and ad-hoc unbundled E2E).

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Review sweep (Claude Fable session): two reporting defects fixed in committed fln-conformance code. (1) ledger.rs validate_fixtures rendered the row INDEX as 'PARITY_LEDGER.txt:{line}' — a missing-fixture error pointed at the wrong line for any ledger with comments/blank lines. Row now carries its 1-based source line and validate_fixtures reports it (regression: ghost-fixture error asserts line 2). (2) normalize.rs compare(): when transcripts differed only below line granularity (e.g. trailing newline), the finding read 'length divergence: ours N lines, oracle N lines' with equal N — self-contradictory triage output. Now reports 'sub-line divergence (line endings or trailing bytes): ours X bytes, oracle Y bytes' (regression added). Detection behavior unchanged in both cases — divergences were already caught; only the rendered findings were wrong. Gates green (fmt/clippy -D warnings/tests/UBS 0 critical, isolated target).

comments.author: CopperHare
comments.text: Shared-index coordination note: peer-staged conformance changes to crates/fln-conformance/src/{ledger.rs,normalize.rs} and the then-current .beads snapshot were included in commit c9c2cde when my narrow fln-8mj lockfile commit raced an already-populated index. No peer content was reverted or dropped; subsequent uncommitted peer edits remain in the tree. The commit subject mentions only fln-8mj, so treat this comment as attribution: ledger source-line diagnostics and equal-line-count byte-divergence reporting in c9c2cde belong to the fln-euo/p8a conformance review work and its separately recorded tests.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Follow-up (Claude Fable session): poison_compile_out.rs had the same scan-coverage gap as domain_enforcement.rs — build.rs/benches/examples were not walked, so an ORACLE_FALLBACK leak could hide there. Both scanners now cover every place cargo compiles code from. Gates green.

comments.author: RubyForest
comments.text: Fresh review of 9c8406b: poison_compile_out.rs expanded per-crate target directories but still enumerates only workspace/crates, so compiled Rust under workspace/tools (currently tools/structure-guard) is outside the ORACLE_FALLBACK scan. The workspace-wide compile-out claim therefore remains open. Please drive the scan from the reviewed workspace member inventory and add a seeded tools-member leak regression.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Minor review notes on scripts/tribunal (Claude Fable session, no edits since the epoch lab is immutable-once-published): (1) gen_epoch_manifest.sh iterates 'for exe in $TOOLCHAIN_DIR/bin/*' — bash glob order follows the host locale's collation, while the manifest claims no-host-dependence; LC_ALL=C is applied to oracle invocations but not the generator shell itself. ASCII-only names make drift unlikely but setting LC_ALL=C at script top (or piping through sort) would make the executable-inventory order provably host-independent before any future regeneration ceremony. (2) Cosmetic: --check modes of gen_epoch_manifest.sh and gen_core_fixtures.sh leak their mktemp scratch on both pass and fail. ref_vs_ref.sh itself reviewed clean.

title: W3: fln check-olean — the Independent Judge CLI and the G1 evidence bundle
description:
Ship fln check-olean — the Independent Judge (§8.6, G1): the standalone auditor that re-checks an entire olean set (all of mathlib in minutes) on a kernel that shares no code, no compiler, and no bignum with the thing it is checking — the program FIRST public artifact, shipped before FrankenLean has elaborated a single file.

Composition: Grimoire read (full-recheck validation level) + Crucible K1/K2 + fln-checker per consensus policy + receipts + optional certificate emission. CLI contract: stable exit codes, --json output (versioned schema), per-declaration verdicts with stable error classes, a summary receipt binding the input set hash, engine build hashes, policy, and totals; --receipts writes the receipt set + transparency-log append. Performance: PG-1 — full mathlib olean set <= 5 min on the 32-core profile, <= 25 min single-thread.

This bead includes the G1 gate evidence assembly: zero acceptance divergence against the Reference toolchain across the full mathlib olean set at the pin, every divergence (if any) triaged under the §8.2 oracle-or-spec-investigation-first law. Acceptance: G1 declared with the evidence bundle (run logs, receipts, Parity Ledger rows, PG-1 numbers on the baseline machine). Tests: CLI transcript fixtures (exit codes, --json schema); interrupted-run drills (partial output never masquerades as complete); determinism — two runs produce identical receipts.

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.

Release-level evidence uses the no-mock lane (real Reference binary/artifact/filesystem/editor/process/host as applicable). title: W7: Anvil & Verdict — simp core, rewrite indexes, owned SAT, grind
description:
Workstream W7 "Anvil & Verdict" (§12): rewrite and decision engines under the single constitutional rule FL-INV-06 — every engine is untrusted; every output enters the environment only through a kernel-checked artifact. That rule buys unlimited freedom below it.

Scope: simp faithful core (simp-set discipline, discrimination-tree retrieval, congruence interplay, dsimp/simp-only behaviors) implemented natively end-to-end behind obs-class Mirror facade rows, fidelity-tested against Corpus simp-heavy files + instrumented-oracle rewrite traces; compiled per-library rewrite indexes shipped as content-addressed Ledger artifacts (staleness impossible by construction); norm_num/omega native cores (fln-bignum-backed, certificate-producing, kernel-replayed); Verdict — owned CDCL SAT (watched literals, VSIDS/CHB, modest honest inprocessing) with OWNED proof logging and an OWNED independent certificate checker so bv_decide sheds the external CaDiCaL TCB (PG-9: 100% agreement, wall <= 2x Reference at G5, ratcheting); grind reimplemented natively (congruence closure, e-matching, theory hooks) entering at L1/L2 and ratcheting to L3 on the grind corpus by G4 — its semantics still move upstream, the census pins the coupling per epoch; e-graph saturation lane (frontier; honest scoping — conditional rewrites and binder-crossing rules restrict the eligible subset; completeness fallback to the faithful core; PG-A measured, not assumed); speculative tactic portfolios (frontier). New lanes promote only by shadow-run matrix protocol (§12.6/§18.9) with auto-demotion on breach.

## Success Criteria

All scope and outcomes stated above remain mandatory, and every dependency/child deliverable required for this epic or gate must be completed at its honest evidence level. The MANDATORY EPIC/GATE CLOSEOUT CONTRACT in Notes must pass with coverage mapping, no-mock E2E evidence, detailed archived human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, reproducible environment facts, and every skip/divergence classified. No feature, compatibility surface, invariant, performance gate, or release claim may be silently narrowed, dropped, or replaced by a prototype.
design: REVIEW AMENDMENT — full W7 closure includes franken_lean-engine-backed-tactic-integration-c90. Engine implementations are not considered user-compatible merely because their direct APIs pass; they must execute through the production Mirror facade and Golem-hosted tactic path, preserve TacticM state/InfoTree behavior, and kernel-check every result. Ledger publication of compiled rewrite indexes remains a W8 integration owned by fln-publish-rewrite-indexes-gca.
notes:
MANDATORY EPIC/GATE CLOSEOUT CONTRACT:
This epic/gate may close only when every child deliverable is complete; the verification registry maps every implemented public behavior, FL-INV claim, Parity-Ledger row, Behavior Note, and PG/gate claim to named unit/property/mutation coverage plus a shared no-mock E2E scenario; detailed human + schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts are archived; all divergences and skips are classified; no mock-only evidence is used for a release claim; and the final evidence bundle records exact reproduction commands, environment/epoch/mode/platform/thread/seed/cache facts, input hashes, receipts/logical roots, expected final states, first divergences, and cleanup results.

title: W1: UPSTREAM_LEDGER + suite upgrade protocol — deliberate, Tribunal-diffed lock bumps
description:
Establish UPSTREAM_LEDGER.md + the suite upgrade protocol (§2.11, R16): primitives that belong in a foundation crate land THERE, tracked per-entry — seed entries from the plan: asupersync (deterministic work-stealing merge combinator — ordered join of speculative results — if the existing quorum/pipeline combinators do not already express it), franken_networkx (incremental dominator maintenance for dynamic DAGs — the live-edit invalidation cone), frankensearch (incremental index commit hooks matching Ledger transaction boundaries), franken_markdown (the Lean lexer profile for the shared highlighter), frankensqlite (any blob-store access pattern the CAS proves out). fln-bignum stays in-repo (kernel-adjacent code answers to the kernel covenant) with the recorded revisit trigger: a second suite member needing arbitrary precision.

The suite upgrade protocol (R16 mitigation): SUITE.lock upgrades are deliberate and Tribunal-diffed — an upgrade PR must carry the closure-audit delta (any new transitive dep is a reviewed allowlist change, D1), the conformance run against the new pin, and a rollback note; per-component acceptance gates before a suite feature becomes load-bearing; if a needed component is immature under this load, the fallback is extracting the narrow algorithm into an fln-* crate (design-level reuse) or rejecting the component — the only-Franken rule holds.

Acceptance: UPSTREAM_LEDGER.md exists with the seed entries, statuses, and owning-repo links; the upgrade-protocol checklist is enforced by CI on any SUITE.lock diff (a lock change without the audit artifacts fails). Tests: the CI enforcement check itself (a seeded bare lock bump must fail with the missing-artifact list logged).

title: W10: native_decide Tier 2 — trace-guided kernel replay discharges ofReduceBool
description:
Implement native_decide Tier 2 — trace-guided kernel replay (§11.6): on certified paths, ofReduceBool is DISCHARGED, not trusted — the Tier 1 replay receipt (function-entry digests over the registered reflection set, branch decisions, literal-arithmetic transcripts) is replayed through Crucible as the DEFAULT for certified profiles, so the compiler and VM leave the trusted base for every evaluation the tier covers.

Scope and honesty rules: the tier holds only where the receipt covers the full evaluation (the registered reflection set; evaluations escaping it remain Tier 1 and are marked so); the trust ledger records per build which tier was achieved, because "we no longer trust the compiler" is a claim with a scope; the cost multiple of replay vs native evaluation is measured and published (an OBSERVED number, per §20.4 — never asserted); Tier 3 (a verified evaluator proven against the Judgment Specification) remains explicitly research and is NOT this bead. Certified --reproducible builds enable Tier 2 by default; standard builds keep Tier 1 sampling policy.

Acceptance: the reflection corpus discharges through kernel replay on certified profiles with zero trust extension to Golem/Iron for covered evaluations; the trust ledger and fln audit --tcb reflect the tier per declaration; the published cost-multiple table lands in the evidence bundle for G6. Tests: e2e certified-build test where a native_decide theorem verifies with the compiler deliberately perturbed (a wrong compiled result MUST be caught by replay — the whole point, proven by mutation); receipt-coverage boundary fixtures (an evaluation outside the registered set must degrade to Tier 1 with the degradation logged and marked, never silently claimed as Tier 2); replay determinism across the certified matrix.

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. title: W7: Frontier lanes — e-graph saturation + tactic portfolios, shadow-run promotion
description:
Implement the frontier rewrite lanes (§12.3, §12.7): the e-graph equality-saturation lane over simp-set subsets with proof extraction (rewrites recorded as justifications, extracted proofs replayed through the ordinary lemma-application path, kernel-checked like everything else — honestly scoped: conditional rewrites and binder-crossing rules restrict the eligible subset; the lane is an accelerator with a completeness fallback to the faithful core, never a replacement; its win condition is MEASURED via the PG-A profile, not assumed), and speculative tactic portfolios (first-with-work-stealing, deterministic winner selection by registered CGSE policy, losers cancelled through their asupersync regions; snapshot-cheap try trees — the search-side dividend of O(1) environment snapshots, exposed as tactics and through Envoy).

Both lanes promote ONLY by shadow-run (§12.6/§18.9): dual-run on the same demand nodes, products diffed, divergence and latency distributions recorded, promotion per workload/epoch/platform cell with deterministic sampling continuing afterward and auto-demotion on any soundness, fidelity, or tail-latency breach — promotion is a matrix, never a global flag. Frontier features never leak into faithful/sound artifacts (D18).

Acceptance: e-graph lane produces kernel-checked results agreeing with the faithful core on its eligible subset, with the measured win condition published; portfolio winner selection deterministic under the lab at any thread count; shadow-run promotion machinery generic enough for the other promotion consumers (K2 optimizations, Iron tiers, epoch bridges). Tests: extraction-proof replay fixtures; eligibility-boundary fixtures (ineligible rewrites must route to the faithful core); cancellation-correctness under portfolio racing (obligation-leak oracle clean); auto-demotion drill (planted breach must demote and file the incident bundle).

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. design: OPEN-QUESTION CLOSEOUT — this bead owns OQ-5. Shadow-run data is evidence, not a substitute for the kernel-checked extraction law.

title: W11: Terminal InfoView + WASM Judge — fln goals in tmux, kernel in a browser tab
description:
Implement the terminal InfoView + the WASM Judge (§16.4, §16.4b): fln goals — the live proof panel over SSH/tmux (where this ecosystem agents and operators actually live), attaching to Lantern over the SAME RPC the editor uses: goals, hover, and widgets as text/ANSI projections via frankentui diff-based rendering; franken_node JS host held as the exploratory path to full widget fidelity outside VS Code (OQ-7 owns the tier decision — wire-compatible pass-through remains the contract).

The WASM Judge (fln-wasm): K1 + fln-checker compiled to a single WASM artifact — sealed-capsule (.flnpack) verification in a browser tab: drag a capsule, watch the kernel re-check it against the same vectors with the same bits; receipt + transparency-log inclusion validation; declaration/dependency browsing over the embedded graph slice. Zero-install third-party verification as a public good — the strongest possible demonstration that the trusted core needs NOTHING from a host: no filesystem, no network, no allocator tricks, no platform libm. Tier-1 scope is the Judge; a full in-browser elaborator is explicitly tier-2-if-ever (OQ-12 owns packaging tier + size/perf budget).

Acceptance: fln goals renders live goal states in tmux against a real daemon session with sub-second update on edits; the WASM Judge verifies a real sealed mathlib-slice capsule in a stock browser with bit-identical vectors vs the native K1 run. Tests: frankentui rendering goldens (ANSI projections); RPC-parity tests (terminal view == editor view for the same snapshot); WASM/native vector-identity CI lane; capsule tamper fixtures (any byte flip must fail verification with a typed error).

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.
design: OPEN-QUESTION CLOSEOUT — this bead owns OQ-12 in addition to OQ-7. Full browser elaboration remains tier-2-if-ever and cannot inflate the Judge's TCB claim.

title: G0: The Laws of the Machine — pre-construction spike gate (§22.1)
description:
Gate G0 "The Laws of the Machine" (§22.1): ten compile-tested spikes and executed decisions that retire the load-bearing unknowns BEFORE any W2-W12 interface freezes. No later gate may begin with its unknowns unresolved (Declaration of Intent commitment 7).

The spikes price the central bets early: ABI resurrection of a real mathlib .olean (feeds Marrow/Grimoire layout methodology), independent kernel check vs lean4checker, Golem execution of real user-class compiled code on ABI values, hygiene/syntax fidelity on macro-heavy corpus files, byte-identical olean re-emit (FL-INV-04 feasibility), heartbeat/fuel-parity calibration, the defeq tail (twenty nastiest Corpus problems on the K2 memoization design), the Mirror-facade viability spike (Bet B2 priced before interfaces freeze), the instrumented-oracle spike (golden decision traces — mitigation for the R2 unifier-fidelity risk), and the full dependency-closure audit with SUITE.lock.

Exit criteria: every spike green or its finding folded into an amended interface decision recorded in the plan (§25 decision log); open questions OQ-1/OQ-2/OQ-3 resolved or explicitly owned by a named workstream. Each spike bead states its own acceptance test. This epic closes only when all ten spike beads close.

## Success Criteria

All scope and outcomes stated above remain mandatory, and every dependency/child deliverable required for this epic or gate must be completed at its honest evidence level. The MANDATORY EPIC/GATE CLOSEOUT CONTRACT in Notes must pass with coverage mapping, no-mock E2E evidence, detailed archived human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, reproducible environment facts, and every skip/divergence classified. No feature, compatibility surface, invariant, performance gate, or release claim may be silently narrowed, dropped, or replaced by a prototype.
notes:
MANDATORY EPIC/GATE CLOSEOUT CONTRACT:
This epic/gate may close only when every child deliverable is complete; the verification registry maps every implemented public behavior, FL-INV claim, Parity-Ledger row, Behavior Note, and PG/gate claim to named unit/property/mutation coverage plus a shared no-mock E2E scenario; detailed human + schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts are archived; all divergences and skips are classified; no mock-only evidence is used for a release claim; and the final evidence bundle records exact reproduction commands, environment/epoch/mode/platform/thread/seed/cache facts, input hashes, receipts/logical roots, expected final states, first divergences, and cleanup results.

title: W9: Lantern & Palimpsest — daemon server, snapshots, causal proof graph
description:
Workstream W9 "Lantern & Palimpsest" (§14, §15): the single-daemon server behind the unchanged wire dialect, and the provenance substrate that makes the system explain itself.

Lantern: LSP + $/lean/* byte-compatible (plainGoal, plainTermGoal, rpc/* sessions, waitForDiagnostics, semantic tokens, widget delivery) — vscode-lean4 connects unmodified; one daemon, one shared immutable import heap (kills the ~60s x N-workers x GBs multiplication; PG-4/PG-6); snapshot machinery: immutable DocumentSnapshots with publication generations (stale-diagnostics race structurally impossible), sub-command incrementality from the finest enclosing checkpoint, cancel-correct under typing bursts, stable-identity diagnostics dedup, idempotent precondition-checked code actions, progressive proof-state negotiation, per-root epoch capsules, session replay bundles (server bugs become deterministic objects); the machine session API — fork a proof state, apply a tactic, read goals, O(1) per branch, thousands of branches per daemon (§14.4, the number that embarrasses the 18-735s per-branch literature).

Palimpsest: always-on ring-buffer traces + opt-in archives (frankensqlite); the causal proof graph (Bet B7) with typed edge families and completeness classes — ONLY Complete/Conservative edges may drive invalidation or trust (the welding law); unification forensics (minimal disagreement), instance why-not trees, semantic blame slices; semantic diff levels + semantic merge (blocks silent semantic conflicts Git cannot see; nothing merges around the kernel); fragility signals (advisory, D3); time travel, fln replay, repro bundles; build forensics as SQL.

Exit: gate G5 — vscode-lean4 smoke matrix green, PG-4/5/6/L met, session API live.

## Success Criteria

All scope and outcomes stated above remain mandatory, and every dependency/child deliverable required for this epic or gate must be completed at its honest evidence level. The MANDATORY EPIC/GATE CLOSEOUT CONTRACT in Notes must pass with coverage mapping, no-mock E2E evidence, detailed archived human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, reproducible environment facts, and every skip/divergence classified. No feature, compatibility surface, invariant, performance gate, or release claim may be silently narrowed, dropped, or replaced by a prototype.
notes:
MANDATORY EPIC/GATE CLOSEOUT CONTRACT:
This epic/gate may close only when every child deliverable is complete; the verification registry maps every implemented public behavior, FL-INV claim, Parity-Ledger row, Behavior Note, and PG/gate claim to named unit/property/mutation coverage plus a shared no-mock E2E scenario; detailed human + schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts are archived; all divergences and skips are classified; no mock-only evidence is used for a release claim; and the final evidence bundle records exact reproduction commands, environment/epoch/mode/platform/thread/seed/cache facts, input hashes, receipts/logical roots, expected final states, first divergences, and cleanup results.

title: W5: Intrinsic table — the extern census implemented natively, row-per-symbol evidence
description:
Implement the intrinsic table from the extern census (§11.4, Appendix C): every @[extern] symbol in Init/Std/Lean at the pin — Nat/Int/Array/ByteArray/String/Float ops, IO, Task, ST, environment access, compiler-API entry points — as native Rust intrinsics beneath the broader Mirror facade. The table is GENERATED from the census (mechanical extraction, W1), and the census diff is the first artifact of every epoch advance.

Semantics: Float arithmetic is IEEE-754 binary64 with the Reference printing/parsing conventions; transcendentals route through fln-libm (§6.8) — no platform libm on any path that can reach an artifact or receipt (D21); UInt*/USize follow the ABI width rules; @[implemented_by] swaps honored at the boundary the ecosystem respects. Every row carries fixtures and a Parity-Ledger L-level; unclassified rows do not exist (census totality).

Acceptance: the census fully populated with an implementation status per row (G2 requirement: fully populated with fixtures); differential parity on the intrinsic fixture corpus vs the Reference (bit class where applicable, obs class where documented); missing-intrinsic calls produce typed unsupported errors naming the row, never UB or a guess. Tests: per-row fixture families generated alongside the table; boundary-value sweeps for numeric ops; String UTF-8 position-convention fixtures; Float printing round-trips.

title: W10: Outbound ABI door — our plugins in the Reference host, per-platform matrix
description:
Implement the outbound ABI door (§17.4, §6.6, risk R5): FrankenLean-built plugins loading into the REFERENCE host — the harder direction of the bidirectional plugin promise, staged deliberately to G5 with its own Tribunal matrix per platform (inbound landed with W2).

Why harder: the Reference host has internal assumptions (allocator behavior, initialization ordering, symbol resolution details) we do not control; our emitted artifacts must satisfy them without any cooperation from upstream. Method: build against the generated ABI contract; validate through the C4 cross-load matrix outbound lanes; every discovered host assumption becomes a contract row (or an honest per-platform tier in the Parity Ledger rather than blocking G5 — the R5 fallback).

Acceptance: FrankenLean-built precompileModules artifacts and @[extern] libraries load and run in the Reference toolchain on the covered platforms; the outbound matrix green per platform or tiered honestly; mixed pipelines (Reference host importing our plugin importing our oleans) exercised. Tests: the outbound C4 lanes per platform; symbol-resolution and initializer-ordering fixtures in the Reference host; a soak with the Reference test suite driving our plugin surfaces.

title: W6: Native tactic framework + bootstrap-safe primitive tactics behind facade rows
description:
Implement the native tactic framework + core tactic set (§10.5): goal management, metavariable assignment, evalTactic dispatch, elab_rules/macro_rules registration and matching, focusing/structuring combinators, evalExpr-class services, and the InfoTree hooks as Athanor code; the core tactic set (rfl-class closing, intro/apply/cases/induction machinery, simp via Anvil, decide via Crucible, omega/bv_decide via Verdict, norm_num-class evaluation) native behind Mirror facade rows — each with equivalence class, golden-trace fixtures, and its own Parity-Ledger L-level.

USER tactics — mathlib, aesop, project elab blocks — run on Golem and call this framework through the facade, receiving GoalState views and capability contexts. No tactic, native or user, can construct a trusted declaration except through Crucible (FL-INV-02). The framework observable semantics (state threading, backtracking, error propagation, InfoTree shape) are conformance surface, proven by the metaprogram-corpus rig running the ecosystem REAL tactic code.

Acceptance: the curated mathlib-tactic slice (G0-8) executes through the production framework with environments, InfoTrees, diagnostics, and generated names diffing clean against the oracle at declared L-levels; core tactics pass their per-row fixture families. Tests: tactic-framework state-threading property tests; backtracking/error-propagation fixtures; facade-row differential families per core tactic; FL-INV-02 mutation test (a tactic attempting direct environment admission must be structurally impossible — compile-time — or caught).

design: REVIEW AMENDMENT — W6 owns goal management, state threading, backtracking, evalTactic dispatch, InfoTree hooks, facade contracts, direct kernel-backed decide/rfl-class behavior, and the bootstrap-safe primitive tactic subset needed to reach G3. It defines stable typed dispatch seams for simp, norm_num, omega, bv_decide, and grind but must not fake those engines before W7. Engine-backed rows remain honest L0/L1 with precise typed Unsupported results until franken_lean-engine-backed-tactic-integration-c90 promotes them. G3's curated metaprogram slice reports those levels explicitly; full engine-backed tactic functionality is preserved and completed before G4.

title: G0-8: Mirror-facade viability spike — census + stubs vs real mathlib tactics
description:
G0-8 Mirror-facade viability spike (§22.1-8, feeds §4.3/§18.2): generate the builtin census and facade stubs for a metaprogram-heavy slice (Lean.Meta core + the tactic-framework entry points); compile a curated set of REAL mathlib tactic files against the facade with native services stubbed at prototype depth; enumerate every API whose semantics resist native backing and assign each an honest L-level.

This is the plan CENTRAL bet (B2) priced before interfaces freeze, and the direct mitigation of Critical risk R1 (Meta-API dependence effectively unbounded — packages compile against Lean.* internals that may expose object identity or allocation order the native facade cannot reproduce). The census partition (toolchain-api native / library-code source-elaborated / user-facing-data) must prove TOTAL on the slice: no unclassified symbol, ever — a symbol the census cannot classify blocks the L-level, never gets guessed.

Acceptance: (a) census extraction runs mechanically over the pinned Init/Std/Lean sources for the slice and emits symbol, signature, telescope, effect class, partition class; (b) generated facade stubs compile against real mathlib tactic code; (c) a written resistance list: every symbol whose observable semantics leak implementation detail, each with equivalence class (bit/obs/perf), honest L-level, and Behavior Note candidate; (d) the ordered closure plan for the facade L-level ratchet (resolves OQ-3). Tests: the slice + tactic files seed the metaprogram-corpus rig (§18.2 Mirror conformance).

title: W2: Marrow object model + tri-state RC — the lean_object ABI twin
description:
Implement the Marrow object model + tri-state reference counting (§6.1, §6.1b, §6.2; crates fln-rt + fln-unsafe-abi): the Reference runtime object model, exactly, under the exported lean_* names — object header (RC word, memory-kind/other byte, tag byte), constructor objects with scalar-field packing, arrays/scalar arrays/strings (UTF-8 with byte-length/capacity semantics), closures (arity, fixed args), thunks, tasks, IO.Refs, external objects with class vtables, bignum objects (fln-bignum-backed mpz-shim view), small Nats as odd tagged pointers ((n << 1) | 1). Every layout constant comes from the W1 generated contract tables.

Two heaps, one membrane: CompatHeap (exact ABI, membrane-only allocation, all unsafe confined to fln-unsafe-abi with ledger rows per site) vs NativeHeap (typed asupersync region handles); generated converters with declared ownership and failure semantics; lazy boundary-specific conversion (a metaprogram that never inspects a term never forces its Compat projection — memory-doubling risk R10 is the named gate PG-6, not an accident). Tri-state RC: single-threaded fast path, atomic shared, persistent-never-counted — matching upstream so ABI-crossing borrow/inc/dec behaves identically. Hardened builds: debug ownership shadows (double-release + foreign-pointer misuse detection, quarantine, deterministic replay events).

Structural laws (D3): no fln-unsafe-* crate depends on fln-kernel/fln-checker; no unsafe export launderable into a checked declaration; panic law at the ABI boundary (§6.5). Acceptance: G0-1/G0-3 findings folded; C4 probes pass both directions on the implemented subset. Tests: layout unit tests generated FROM the contract module; RC balance property tests; ownership-shadow mutation tests.
