comments.author: CopperHare
comments.text: Required follow-on from fln-8mj hardening: the bootstrap structure guard now fails closed on every externally public item/symbol and on boundary-crate macro definitions/invocations, because textual scanning cannot prove that macro expansion will not synthesize an unledgered allow(unsafe_code) or export. This is intentionally safe for the stub phase but cannot become an accidental feature loss. Before Marrow exposes its required ABI, this bead must replace the scaffold rule with an expansion-aware/type-aware no-admission export covenant: classify the fully expanded public Rust API and exported symbol table; prove return/output types cannot launder into CheckedExpr/Declaration/environment admission; map every allowed symbol to generated ABI contracts and unsafe-ledger evidence; reject macro-generated or cfg-dependent unknowns; and seed laundering, macro-generated allow/export, no_mangle/export_name/global_asm, generic/associated-type, feature-matrix, and recovery cases. The replacement must preserve D3 while admitting every required lean_* ABI surface.

title: W10: Iron backends — owned native codegen + JIT, C backend as parity apparatus
description:
Implement the Iron backends (§17.3, §11.3): the sovereign path — FLBC to native objects/executables via an owned baseline code generator (NO LLVM, no cranelift, no cc), x86-64 + aarch64 first, so lake build of executables needs no external toolchain (the G6 headline; resolves weakness census #12 partially and the mandatory-cc contract entirely). Iron-JIT (frontier, standard-mode only, fln-unsafe-jit): a baseline template JIT from FLBC with inline caches burned in — the tier that makes hot tactic loops approach precompiled speed; certified builds never enable it; results bit-identical to the interpreter by construction, spot-checked by a standing dual-run rig (PG-7: JIT >= 0.5x Reference-precompiled on the corpus).

Also: --backend c — emit C in the Reference idiom and drive the USER toolchain exactly as upstream leanc does (D2 tool #1, optional), for parity studies and exotic targets; never the anchor (D16). Both backends dual-run governed: Iron pinned to interpreter results, interpreter pinned to Reference observable evaluation semantics. OQ-4 (target set beyond x86-64/aarch64; JIT/static emitter sharing) is resolved inside this bead and recorded in the decision log.

Acceptance: real executables (incl. a Lake-built project binary) produced by Iron run correctly with zero cc present; JIT dual-run rig green; C-backend output compiles under the Reference leanc contract on the parity corpus. Tests: differential execution interpreter-vs-Iron-vs-C-backend on the corpus; executable-page management ledger rows audited (fln-unsafe-jit, D3); JIT-vs-interpreter bit-identity spot checks in CI; cross-arch CI lanes.

title: Gate G1: The Independent Judge — all of mathlib re-checked, zero divergence, PG-1
description:
Convergence gate G1 "The Independent Judge" (§22.3): Marrow + Grimoire + Crucible check EVERY mathlib olean at the pin with zero acceptance divergence against the Reference toolchain; PG-1 met (<= 5 min on the 32-core profile, <= 25 min single-thread); fln check-olean ships as a standalone auditor.

Why this gate is first and why it matters strategically: the program FIRST public artifact is the strongest independent verification the Lean world has ever had — a foreign-blooded kernel (no shared code, no shared compiler, no shared bignum) re-checking the entire corpus in minutes — shipped BEFORE FrankenLean has elaborated a single file. It is simultaneously the deepest de-risking event available: it proves the ABI/olean/kernel spine (the layout extraction methodology, the region codec, the judgment inventory) against the full Corpus at production scale.

Evidence bundle required to declare the gate: full-corpus run logs on the baseline machine; receipts + transparency log entries; the kernel differential rigs green (lean4checker/lean4lean agreement); Parity Ledger rows for the kernel/olean/ABI surfaces at their achieved L-levels; PG-1 and PG-8 numbers with claim states; every divergence (if any occurred) closed through the §8.2 triage law with fixtures.

title: W8: Publish compiled rewrite indexes — Ledger CAS + olean-next attachment integration
description:
Integrate W7's deterministic compiled rewrite-index format with the W8 Ledger and Grimoire artifact planes. Publish each per-library discrimination automaton as a typed demand-node product and olean-next attachment keyed by the exact simp-set semantic hash, implementation/schema version, epoch, mode, and CGSE policy; hydrate it through local/remote CAS validation; project it into daemon sessions without rebuilding; and invalidate or reject it structurally whenever any key input changes.

This bead resolves the stage boundary cleanly: W7 implements and proves the faithful simp driver plus deterministic index builder/reader equivalence; W8 owns transactional publication, retention, hydration, invalidation, quarantine, and cache provenance. No W7 component may claim a Ledger-published artifact before this integration exists.

Acceptance: a mathlib slice builds indexes once, publishes atomically, reloads from a fresh process and from a validated remote-cache fixture, and yields candidate sets and simp results identical to a fresh in-memory index; unchanged simp sets reuse byte-identical artifacts; changed rules/options/epoch/schema cannot load stale artifacts; promotion crashes leave no half-published attachment.

Verification: unit schema/key tests, builder-vs-loaded representation equivalence, stale-key and poisoned-cache mutations, kill-at-every-promotion-step drills, and shared no-mock E2E scripts for build -> publish -> hydrate -> simplify with complete CAS keys, manifests, hashes, receipts, expected/final states, and first-divergence logs.

title: W6: Unifier/defeq — the approximation ladder, implemented against golden traces
description:
Implement unification + defeq against golden traces (§10.2): transparency-aware whnf/isDefEq mirroring the Reference approximation ladder — syntactic fast paths, first-order approximation, Miller patterns, delayed assignments, postponement, unfolding by reducibility and definitional height, structure-eta awareness. Mathlib DEPENDS on the precise power of the unifier (proofs elaborate because a particular approximation fires); this is where bug-for-bug earns its keep and where Critical risk R2 lives.

Method (the R2 mitigation): implemented AGAINST the instrumented-oracle golden decision traces (G0-9 -> §18.3) — which approximation fired, what postponed, what unfolded, in what order — at Corpus scale, with trace-replay rigs running continuously. Every T3 divergence is triaged with the strong prior that it is a fidelity defect; a persistent divergence class becomes a faithful-mode blocker and a published erratum with quantified Corpus blast radius, never a quiet Behavior Note (the epoch ships R3, not R4, until closed). Failures are recorded as typed constraint cells (HasType, IsDefEq, SynthesizeInstance, Coerce, ResolveName, ElaboratePattern, ProveTermination) with origins, dependencies, attempts, and outcomes — the substrate Palimpsest blame slices are computed from.

Acceptance: trace-replay agreement on the pilot corpus, expanding toward Corpus scale with the divergence ledger empty or fully classified; the G0-7 tail suite green through the production engine; constraint-cell recording complete per the perturbation validator. Tests: golden-trace replay rigs per approximation stage; postponement-order fixtures; defeq differentials vs the oracle on C0-C2.

title: W6: Term elaboration — coercions, match compilation, recursion elaborators, exact generated names
description:
Implement term elaboration (§10.3): coercion insertion, implicit/strict-implicit/instance-implicit binder handling, auto-bound implicits, structure instances and anonymous constructors, dependent match compilation into auxiliary matchers with generated equation lemmas and splitters MATCHING the Reference generated-name and shape conventions (metaprograms pattern-match on these artifacts — they are API, not internals), deriving handler dispatch, attribute application, variable/section/namespace resolution with the Reference ambiguity rules, and the recursion elaborators (structural, well-founded with the same measure-synthesis surface, partial, unsafe) lowering to EXACTLY the kernel-visible constructions of §1.2 (recursors, WellFounded.fix, unfold equations — the kernel sees only these).

Termination elaboration records a machine-readable TerminationCard (call graph, candidate measures, generated obligations, chosen theorem dependencies) — the artifact the Bloodhound repair lane consumes when a termination proof breaks under migration (§16.1).

Acceptance: generated-name conventions byte-agree with the oracle on the match/equation-lemma corpus (C2 metaprogramming-weight packages); recursion lowerings kernel-check identically; TerminationCards emitted and schema-validated. Tests: match-compilation differentials incl. nested/overlapping patterns; equation-lemma shape fixtures; deriving-handler corpus; ambiguity-resolution fixtures per the Reference rules.

title: W1: Typed error taxonomy + faithful diagnostic renderers — D8 values, R9 epoch projection
description:
Implement the typed error taxonomy + the faithful diagnostic renderers (D8 normative taxonomy, risk R9): errors cross crate boundaries as TYPED, VERSIONED VALUES — SyntaxFailure, MacroFailure, ElaborationFailure, KernelRejection, KernelInconclusive (resources/cancellation, FL-INV-07), ArtifactCorrupt, ArtifactEpochMismatch, AbiViolation, CapabilityDenied, PluginCrashed, BuildFailure, ProtocolFailure, ReplayDivergence, InternalInvariantViolation — defined once in fln-core, consumed everywhere. Panics are invariant failures, never user diagnostics; malformed source, artifacts, protocol messages, and plugin output must not panic anywhere in the workspace.

The renderer half (R9: faithful diagnostic text/ordering can depend on incidental internal traversal order upstream): faithful-mode frontends render the typed values into Reference-compatible text and JSON via a per-EPOCH renderer — the typed causal model is the source of truth, the renderer is a projection, and receipts retain the typed cause even when the rendered text is upstream-identical. Message TEXT is equivalence-classed per §4.1 (the Parity Ledger carries separate semantic-vs-exact rows); where exact text depends on incidental upstream order, the row holds at L2/L3 (semantic) with the exact-renderer gap NAMED, and CGSE policies pin our own rendering order. Sound mode renders the richer Palimpsest bodies (BN-02) with positions and severities unchanged.

Acceptance: the taxonomy crate compiles with exhaustive match enforcement (adding a variant breaks every consumer until handled — no catch-all arms in authoritative crates); the faithful renderer reproduces Reference diagnostics on the transcript corpus at each row declared class (exact or semantic); every panic path in a fuzz sweep of malformed inputs surfaces as a typed value, never an abort. Tests: renderer golden tests against the C-family diagnostic transcript corpus with per-row comparison classes logged; a taxonomy-versioning test (an old serialized error decodes or fails typed, never garbles); panic-freedom fuzzing across every decoder entry point; equivalence-class regression tests so a wording change in sound mode can never leak into faithful output.

title: W1: Mode system + certified axis — structural faithful/sound/frontier, D18 leak prevention
description:
Implement the mode system + determinism/evidence axis plumbing (§4.2, D17, D18): the machinery that makes faithful / sound / frontier a STRUCTURAL property of every artifact rather than a flag convention.

Scope: mode selection threading through every subsystem (one type, one source of truth, no per-crate booleans); the orthogonal determinism axis — standard (FL-INV-01 always holds) vs certified (--reproducible: the complete content-hashed input closure — sources, toolchain, SUITE.lock, options, plugin hashes, mode, CGSE policy epoch — yields bit-identical artifacts across the certified matrix, with the sidecar closure manifest; D2 tool products excluded from certification BY CONSTRUCTION); the evidence axis wiring (per-surface L-levels and per-release R-levels read from the Parity Ledger, never asserted). The D18 leak-prevention law is the heart: frontier features NEVER leak into faithful/sound artifacts — every artifact carries its producing mode in provenance, faithful/sound artifact sets are CI-scanned for frontier products (a frontier-marked object in a sound artifact set is a build break), and frontier-only code paths (olean-next emission, e-graph lanes, Iron-JIT, MCP write-tools, epoch bridges) are gated at compile time or capability level, not by if-statements convention.

Why this is its own bead: a dozen other beads say "faithful mode does X, sound mode does Y, frontier never leaks" — this bead builds the mechanism they all assume, early enough that no subsystem invents its own mode plumbing. Acceptance: mode + axis types land in fln-core with the provenance-marking scheme; the leak-scan CI check catches a seeded frontier artifact in a sound set; a --reproducible fixture build produces the sidecar manifest and bit-identical outputs on two runs. Tests: mode-propagation property tests (no code path observes a mode other than the session mode); leak-scan mutation tests; certified-closure completeness test (omitting any closure input from the manifest fails); Behavior-Note linkage checks (every sound-vs-faithful divergence point names its BN id, CI-verified against the register).

title: W1: Tribunal campaign machinery — mutation, fuzzing, drills, delta-debugger, thread matrix
description:
Build the Tribunal campaign machinery as a reusable framework (§18.2, §18.3) — the apparatus the per-subsystem rigs plug into, distinct from the W1 bootstrap (epoch lab + differential harness):

(1) The mutation-campaign framework: seeded-defect injection (skipped positivity check, inverted universe condition, leaked child-transaction assignment, omitted effect read, corrupted olean bounds check, dropped retain, bypassed capability, stale publication generation accepted, cache receipt trusted without hash validation) with the kill-tracking ledger — every critical mutant must be killed by a NAMED test or check; a surviving critical mutant blocks the gate. (2) The fuzzing fleet: grammar-based Lean source generation (syntax-directed + Corpus mutation), kernel term storms, region/olean structure fuzzing, VM opcode fuzzing — all under budgets, all crash-triaged to fixtures automatically. (3) The fault-and-recovery drill framework: kill -9 at every CAS/manifest promotion step, corrupted local/remote cache objects, disk-full mid-build, compiler-child crashes, server crashes with coordinator restart — each drill declaring an EXPECTED FINAL STATE and proving which products committed and which rolled back ("the process restarted" is not a pass). (4) The delta-debugger: automatic shrinking of any divergence or crash into a minimal checked-in fixture. (5) The no-mock lane enforcement: release-level claims close only against real binaries, real filesystems, real editors, real corruption — the evidence gate rejects mocked boundaries. (6) The thread-matrix determinism runner: {1,8,32} per commit, {96}+ weekly, diffing environments/diagnostics/artifacts bit-for-bit (PG-5 infrastructure).

Acceptance: each framework demonstrated on a seeded target with detailed structured logging; the kill-ledger, drill-expectation, and no-mock policies are machine-checked in CI, not conventions. Tests: framework self-tests (planted mutant survives -> gate blocks; drill final-state mismatch -> failure with the state diff logged).

title: W7: grind reimplementation — congruence + e-matching, honestly staged L-levels
description:
Reimplement grind natively (§12.6): grind is core-toolchain machinery at the pin, so under the Oracle-Only Law it is reimplemented — congruence closure, e-matching, and its theory hooks as Anvil engines behind facade rows — developed against instrumented-oracle decision traces and Reference-as-oracle differentials.

Staging is deliberate and honest: its Parity-Ledger rows ENTER at L1/L2 and ratchet to L3 on the grind corpus by G4 rather than pretending day-one exactness, because grind semantics are still moving upstream and the census pins our coupling per epoch. Mathlib-layer automation (aesop, polyrith, ...) is user code on Golem and needs NO reimplementation. All outputs kernel-checked per FL-INV-06.

Acceptance: the grind corpus at L3 by G4 (goal-closure agreement with classified exclusions); decision-trace replay green on the covered fragment; epoch-diff tooling flags upstream grind changes as census deltas requiring row review. Tests: congruence-closure property tests (equivalence-class correctness vs a simple oracle implementation); e-matching trigger fixtures from the traces; differential goal families with the exclusion ledger explicit.

title: W2: Compacted regions — construction, mmap load, relocation, page sharing
description:
Implement compacted regions (§6.4): region construction (object-graph serialization with pointer relocation tables), region loading (mmap, relocate-or-copy fallback, page-sharing across consumers — the enabler of the Lantern shared import heap §7.5), and region hygiene (immutability enforcement, persistent-RC marking).

Design law: this is deliberately THE SAME code path the Grimoire olean codec uses — olean fidelity and runtime-region fidelity are one test surface, so every codec round-trip test also exercises the runtime region machinery and vice versa. mmap and arena primitives beyond what asupersync region heaps provide live in fln-unsafe-region with ledger rows (D3).

Acceptance: a real mathlib olean region loads via mmap with relocation and is traversable as live objects (G0-1 promotion to production code); page-sharing verified across two consumers of one loaded region (RSS accounting shows shared pages, the PG-4/PG-6 mechanism); immutability violations trap in hardened builds. Tests: relocate-or-copy fallback forced and verified; corrupted-relocation-table fuzzing under resource budgets (hostile olean is a DoS surface even in safe Rust — R18); crash-during-construction drills leaving no half-published region (atomic staging).

title: W1: Windows functional CI — early platform honesty, certification deferred
description:
Stand up windows-x86-64 functional CI from W1 (§18.3, D17): windows is FUNCTIONAL from the start (build, unit tests, the growing conformance subset), while bit-certification under --reproducible remains a separate declared decision made later (the manim precedent).

Why early: platform assumptions fossilize fast — path handling, UTF-16 position conversions at the LSP boundary, dlopen vs LoadLibrary in the plugin door, mmap semantics in region loading, process spawning in the D2 subprocess protocol. Discovering these at W12 would mean re-opening frozen interfaces. The certified matrix (linux-x86-64, linux-aarch64, macos-aarch64) runs the determinism closure; windows runs the functional lanes and reports honestly in the Parity Ledger platform column.

Acceptance: CI matrix includes windows building the workspace and running the unit + conformance-smoke lanes; platform-conditional code carries explicit capability errors (never silent substitution); the Parity Ledger platform column reports windows rows at their honest L-levels. Tests: the CI lane itself, plus a fixture asserting that platform-gated features fail closed with typed CapabilityDenied errors rather than degrading silently.

title: W5: FIR pipeline — validated IR stages, pass witnesses, RC/FBIP disciplines
description:
Implement the FIR pipeline (fln-comp, §11.1): elaborated terms -> FIR (ANF/LCNF-class IR keeping the Reference-established compilation disciplines: specialization of instance/closed arguments, simplification, closure conversion, explicit boxing, reference-count insertion with borrow inference and constructor-reuse/FBIP analysis — kept because FBIP-style reuse is the performance model the ecosystem code is written against and is observable through performance cliffs) -> FLBC register bytecode.

Engineering law: every pipeline stage has a versioned IR schema, a deterministic printer, and a validator; every optimization pass is a pure ValidatedIr -> (ValidatedIr, PassWitness) transformation with declared preconditions, a preservation argument or executable validator, a resource-growth bound, deterministic ordering, and a fallback to its input. The RC-insertion pass runs an ownership validator (no use-after-consume, balanced joins and exception edges, capture correctness, ABI ownership contracts) deliberately SIMPLER than the optimizer it audits. Executable-closure selection records WHY each declaration is retained (direct call, closure capture, initializer, reflection, exported symbol, conservative opaque edge) so dead-code questions have answers.

Acceptance: the G0-3 micro-corpus compiles through the full pipeline with every pass witness validating; compiled results observably identical to interpretation of the unoptimized form (pass-preservation differential). Tests: IR printer/parser round-trips; ownership-validator mutation tests (a planted dropped-retain must be caught); pass-ordering determinism at {1,8,32} threads.

title: W9: Forensics, semantic diff/merge, why-trusts, time travel, SQL
description:
Implement the forensics + semantic diff/merge surfaces (§15.2, §15.3, §15.3b, §15.3c, §15.4, §15.5): unification forensics (sound-mode failures report the MINIMAL DISAGREEMENT — smallest subterm pair, transparency in force, approximation stage that gave up, assignment trail — rendered as a diff, not a wall; faithful renders upstream text verbatim); instance forensics (the Synod why-not tree with a one-line head above the expandable tree); both projections of one mechanism — the semantic blame slice (minimal or near-minimal-honestly-labeled causal subgraph, stable node ids: robots get the typed slice, humans get the rendering).

Semantic diff (fln diff): levels source -> syntax -> interface -> proof -> reduction -> extension -> executable -> evidence, so "proof-only change" is a CHECKABLE claim. Semantic merge: branches merge automatically when the graph proves their transactions commute AND the combined declarations re-check through Crucible; text-conflict-but-semantically-independent proposes the patch; text-clean-but-semantics-collide (instance/simp/notation effects) BLOCKS the silent merge and explains — the failure mode Git cannot see; nothing merges around the kernel. Fragility signals (§15.3c): advisory-only (D3), constitutionally barred from Crucible. fln why-trusts: the trust cone walk (axioms, sorryAx, native_decide receipts + tiers, extern/plugin surfaces, --trust extensions). Time travel: goal-state scrubbing, fln replay <decl> from the recorded closure, repro bundles packing a bug input closure. Build forensics: the SQL surface (fln doctor --sql + shipped report views).

Acceptance: forensic outputs validated on curated failure corpora (unification and instance failures with known causes); merge-conflict fixtures demonstrate the semantic-block on Git-clean merges; why-trusts output matches hand-computed cones. Tests: blame-slice minimality checks on seeded contradictions; diff-level property tests (a proof-only edit shows empty interface diff); merge commute-proof mutation tests; replay determinism fixtures.

title: W2: olean codec write + .ilean — byte-identical faithful emission, atomic promotion
description:
Implement the olean codec, write side + .ilean (§7.3, §7.3b): region construction the Reference loads — identical header discipline, identical object layout — and in faithful mode BYTE-IDENTICAL output for FL-INV-04 (read a Reference olean, rebuild, diff bytes = empty; then the harder fresh-elaboration direction for the deterministic subset, residual freedoms pinned by registered CGSE policies per spike G0-5).

Two identities travel with every stored object: semantic hash (canonical meaning under the schema) and byte hash (stored encoding). Cross-file artifact sets (.olean + .ilean + native siblings) promote through a transaction manifest: staged, verified, then published atomically together or rolled back together — a crash mid-promotion leaves unreachable staging, never a half-published module. The .ilean codec is an exact epoch projection (spans, identifier occurrences, resolution targets with the pin field order, omissions, and position conventions) generated from the same normative schema layer as the olean codec so writer and checker cannot skew; richer server metadata lives in Palimpsest — .ilean stays exactly as boring as the ecosystem expects.

Acceptance: FL-INV-04 round-trip green at 100% of the Corpus set (PG-8); Reference toolchain loads and checks our written oleans (mixed-producer matrix seed); kill -9 drills at every promotion step leave recoverable state. Tests: byte-diff rigs per corpus file; .ilean field-order fixtures; the fault drill family (§18.2) for promotion.

title: W9: Palimpsest — event journal, causal proof graph, the welding law
description:
Implement the Palimpsest substrate + causal proof graph (§15.1): structured elaboration events — unifier attempts, instance candidates, macro expansions, scheduler decisions, cache hits, kernel verdicts — recorded per declaration into ring-buffered traces (always-on cheap tier) and full archives (opt-in, frankensqlite-backed), keyed to Syntax ranges. Recording NEVER changes results (observer discipline, enforced by dual-run rigs: traced and untraced runs must produce identical products).

From the journal, the causal proof graph (Bet B7): typed nodes (source ranges, commands, expansions, declarations, instances, searches, tactic steps, kernel/checker verdicts, extension entries, build facets, capability sessions, branches/merges) and typed EDGE FAMILIES (IMPORTS; READS_TYPE vs READS_VALUE vs UNFOLDS; SELECTS_INSTANCE/REJECTS_INSTANCE; USES_SIMP_RULE; EXPANDS_FROM/GENERATED_BY; READS_GRAMMAR/WRITES_GRAMMAR; PROVES/CHECKED_BY/CROSS_CHECKED_BY; COMPILES_TO/EXPORTS_SYMBOL/INITIALIZES_BEFORE; READS_FILE/SPAWNS_TOOL/LOADS_LIBRARY; INVALIDATES/REUSES; SUGGESTED_BY; MIGRATES_TO/REPAIRS), every edge carrying span, phase, policy/epoch, receipt id, and a COMPLETENESS CLASS: Complete (perturbation-tested), Conservative (may over-approximate, must not miss), Observed, Opaque, Inferred.

THE WELDING LAW (stated once, enforced everywhere): only Complete and Conservative edges may drive Ledger invalidation or any trust decision; Observed/Inferred may rank, explain, suggest — never invalidate. Impact cones (interface, definitional, instance/search, syntax, executable, evidence, conservative-potential) computed from deltas — the same cones the Ledger matrix consumes and reviewers read as release risk. Storage: franken_networkx in-process over frankensqlite journals; optional frankengraphdb export is frontier, derived, never load-bearing. Acceptance: observer-discipline dual-run rig green; cone computation validated against the invalidation matrix fixtures; completeness-class enforcement CI check (an Observed edge reaching the invalidation path must be structurally impossible). Tests: journal crash-recovery; graph rebuild-from-journal determinism; export redaction fixtures (§15.3c privacy law).

title: G0-5: Byte-identical olean re-emit spike — enumerate and pin serialization freedoms
description:
G0-5 Byte-identical re-emit spike (§22.1-5, feeds §7.3): read a set of Reference-produced oleans, rebuild the compacted region from the decoded object graph, and byte-diff the output against the original. Then the harder direction: elaborate a module fresh (prototype path) and match the Reference emission for the deterministic subset.

This is the feasibility proof for FL-INV-04 (codec fidelity: read-then-write is byte identity in faithful mode) and directly prices risk R3 (byte-exact write over-constrained by hidden emission nondeterminism). Every serialization freedom found — object traversal order, region layout choices, padding, interning decisions — must be ENUMERATED and pinned with a registered CGSE policy, or (if genuinely nondeterministic upstream) documented as the scoped-down law of R3 (load-compatible + canonical write with the byte law kept for read-then-write).

Acceptance: (a) read->rebuild->bytediff == identical for the fixture set; (b) an exhaustive written enumeration of serialization freedoms with a pinned policy per row; (c) a verdict memo: FL-INV-04 stands as stated, or the R3 fallback scope is invoked with exact wording for the Parity Ledger row. Tests: the round-trip harness becomes the standing codec rig (§18.2 codec rigs) and the FL-INV-04 CI gate at PG-8.

title: W12: Witness claim matrix + docs CI — evidence-gated wording, safe mode, auto-demotion
description:
Implement Witness — the claim-to-proof matrix + documentation CI (§20.4, Bet B8): every public claim (compatibility, soundness, determinism, performance, recovery, security) is a row — claim id, exact text or template, allowed evidence state (OBSERVED with a no-mock reproduction command | TARGETED | HYPOTHESIS | PROVEN with stated scope | BLOCKED | RETIRED), owner workstream, scope (epoch/platform/mode/corpus), required gate + artifact schemas, freshness predicate, forbidden stronger wording, fallback text.

Documentation CI parses the docs against the matrix and REJECTS wording stronger than the evidence permits; coverage counts are generated from the ledgers, never hand-maintained; a benchmark is OBSERVED, never PROVEN; a Crucible-checked theorem is PROVEN relative to the assumptions its receipt names. Companion machinery: SAFE MODE — on InternalFault, checker disagreement, or corruption detection, the affected process fails closed, quarantines its caches, and requires independent revalidation before its outputs are trusted again; AUTO-DEMOTION — a promoted lane breaching thresholds demotes itself and files the incident bundle. The system explains its own behavior with the same rigor it demands of proofs.

Also here: the public Parity Ledger dashboard and the Behavior Notes register (BN-01..BN-08 seeded from §18.8, grown by evidence) as published, generated artifacts. Acceptance: docs CI catches a seeded overclaim (planted wording stronger than the matrix state must fail the build); safe-mode drills quarantine and revalidate; the dashboard regenerates from ledger data alone. Tests: matrix-schema validation; freshness-expiry fixtures (a stale OBSERVED claim must demote to its fallback text); auto-demotion incident-bundle fixtures.

title: W6: Mirror conformance rig — real tactic code differentials, T2/T3 tiers, PG-M
description:
Stand up the Mirror conformance rig — the metaprogram-corpus differential (§18.2): the ecosystem REAL tactic and metaprogram code (corpus family C2: packages chosen for metaprogramming/FFI/Lake/syntax weight) executed on Golem against the native facade, with environments, InfoTrees, diagnostics, and generated names diffed against the oracle runs — nightly, randomized workloads included. This rig is where the census totality claim and every facade row L-level are EARNED, continuously.

Also in scope: the T2/T3 differential elaboration tiers over the Corpus (T2: acceptance + diagnostics positions/severities + statement-level environment identity; T3: term-level identity up to registered normalization — alpha, mdata policy — with residual divergences triaged defect-first); comparison classes as versioned normalizer code; the declaration differential cross-checking both implementations outputs through Crucible AND fln-checker; PG-M measurement (metaprogram corpus wall <= 1.25x Reference at G4, <= 0.9x at G6 — native facade beating hosted dispatch is the design bet, measured not assumed).

Acceptance: the rig runs nightly with a divergence ledger where every entry is classified (fidelity defect with owner, or Behavior-Note candidate with evidence); T2 = 100% on the gated corpus at G4; PG-M trajectory published. Tests: the rig self-tests via seeded facade defects (a perturbed facade row must surface as a divergence, proving detection power); trace-replay integration with the golden-trace lanes.

title: W3: Kernel differential rigs — foreign witnesses, term storms, delta-debugging
description:
Stand up the standing kernel differential rigs (§8.7, §18.2): Crucible vs the Reference kernel (via lean4checker on shared oleans); Crucible vs lean4lean verified rules (fixture translation through the export format); K1 vs K2 continuous; fuzzed-term storms (well-typed generators + mutation) where ANY pairwise disagreement is a finding — the kernel is the one subsystem where we volunteer to be outnumbered.

Triage law (§8.2, R7): a Reference-accepted / Crucible-rejected finding opens an oracle-or-spec INVESTIGATION first — never auto-classified as our defect or theirs; if the Reference accepts a term the type theory rejects (upstream soundness bug — it has happened), FrankenLean matches the THEORY, files the reproducer upstream, and records the Behavior Note (soundness beats bug-parity, D23). Any soundness-side divergence in our direction is a stop-ship publish-the-finding event. Every finding auto-shrinks via the Tribunal delta-debugger into a minimal fixture.

Acceptance: all four rig families running in CI on the fixture corpus + nightly on larger storms; external-witness sampling (lean4checker/lean4lean seats in the §8.3c council on sampled classes) wired; the delta-debugger produces minimal reproducers automatically. Tests: seeded-divergence mutants per rule family must be detected by the rigs (mutation-kill evidence per §18.2); rig determinism (same seed, same storm, same verdicts).

title: W4: Hygiene + macro transactions — stable generated names, memoizable expansion
description:
Implement hygiene + macro expansion transactions (§9.2): macro scopes exactly as the Reference implements them (scope-decorated names, Syntax quotation/antiquotation semantics, macro_rules matching), with generated identifiers derived from STABLE expansion paths (origin command -> macro invocation -> quotation path -> local ordinal) — never thread timing or allocator addresses, because metaprograms and diagnostics observe generated names (determinism of names is a compatibility requirement).

Macro expansion is a TRANSACTION: an invocation receives syntax + hygiene seed + grammar/extension snapshot + declared capabilities + a deterministic budget, and returns expanded syntax, generated names, messages, a source map, extension reads, and capability events. Memoizable exactly when all reads are known: a file-reading macro caches against the capability-recorded canonical path AND content hash; a clock-reading macro is opaque in faithful mode and denied by default in hardened sound profiles.

Acceptance: the G0-4 hygiene rig green on production code; expansion-path name derivation verified stable across thread counts and runs; transaction memoization correctness (cache hit == fresh expansion) under capability-read tracking. Tests: hygiene corner-case corpus (shadowing, splices, cross-macro capture); quotation/antiquotation round-trips; a capability-lying mutant (macro reads a file without declaring it) must be caught by the perturbation validator.

title: Gate G2: The Golem Wakes — user code runs, plugins load, PG-7 floor
description:
Convergence gate G2 "The Golem Wakes" (§22.3): user-class compiled Lean code executes on Golem with #eval/IO/Task parity on the fixture corpus; Reference-built plugins load and run (ABI door, inbound); initialization ordering proven; PG-7 interpreter floor met on early corpora; the intrinsic census fully populated with fixtures.

This gate proves the execution half of the Native Mirror bet: the VM value-identity claim (FLBC values ARE ABI objects — one calling convention across interpreted code, native intrinsics, and foreign plugins) holds for the code class Golem will actually run in production (mathlib tactics, lakefiles, #eval bodies). It is the prerequisite for W6 tactic-framework work at scale and for the Lake surface (lakefile.lean execution).

Evidence bundle: the parity corpus green under both the production effect plane and the lab runtime; real plugin load/run transcripts with initialization-DAG receipts; C4 inbound matrix results per platform; PG-7 measurements against the W1 baseline with claim states; RC-balance and ownership-shadow soak results.

title: W1: Contract extraction — ABI/OLEAN contracts, extern + builtin censuses (generated, never transcribed)
description:
Build the contract-extraction toolchain (Rule D5/D9, Appendix B, Appendix C): checked-in scripts that parse the PINNED Reference sources and emit (a) ABI_CONTRACT.md + a Rust constants module — object header field offsets/widths, tag values per category, size-class and scalar-packing rules, the full exported lean_* function census with signatures and per-parameter ownership conventions, and the per-symbol status taxonomy (NativeSafe / RawPlatform / CompatWrapper / ReferenceSemanticAdapter / Unsupported — no unclassified symbol); (b) OLEAN_CONTRACT.md — header fields, versioning, relocation-table format, ModuleData field inventory, extension-entry encoding, plus the .ilean field contract; (c) the extern census (@[extern] symbols in Init/Std/Lean) feeding the Golem intrinsic table; (d) the builtin census with the Mirror partition class per symbol (toolchain-api / library-code / user-facing-data).

The law: layout constants are DERIVED, never remembered. CI re-runs extraction against the pin on every contract-job run and fails on drift among pin, contract docs, and generated constants. The census diff is the first artifact of every future epoch advance (§22.5).

Acceptance: all four extractions run mechanically from a cold checkout of vendor/lean4-src; generated constants compile; G0-1 consumed them successfully; CI drift job green. Tests: extraction determinism (two runs byte-identical); a seeded-drift mutation (perturb one constant, CI must fail).

## Acceptance Criteria

design: REVIEW AMENDMENT — one canonical extracted inventory feeds every generated surface. ABI layout/tags/exports come from the pinned lean.h and build metadata; olean/ilean layout comes from module.cpp, compact.{h,cpp}, Lean.Environment.ModuleData, and Lean.Server.Ilean; extern and builtin inventories come from a pin-verified Reference environment walk rather than source regex, preserving qualified names, universes, binders, signatures, generated declarations, and attributes. ABI status, Mirror partition, ownership/effect class, and support level are explicit keyed policy inputs joined against the extracted inventory; extraction fails on missing, duplicate, stale, or unknown policy rows. Markdown contracts, Rust constants, façade/census tables, fixtures, and drift reports render from the same canonical intermediate so they cannot disagree by construction. Extraction is offline, read-only over vendor/lean4-src, deterministic under locale/path/time variation, and never executes upstream as a FrankenLean component.
acceptance_criteria: From a cold checkout, all four inventories regenerate offline and byte-identically twice; generated Rust compiles; ABI/OLEAN Markdown and constants share one canonical inventory root; every extracted symbol/field has exactly one reviewed policy row and no stale row; the vendor tree and Reference epoch/commit/tree are cryptographically bound in the evidence. Seeded mutations to a layout constant, symbol signature, ownership/effect class, Mirror partition, olean/ilean field, policy row, pin, and generated output are each killed with exact first-divergence logging, followed by a clean recovery run. Shared no-mock E2E consumes the generated contracts through G0-1 and the intrinsic/facade inputs rather than merely diffing text. scripts/check.sh, contract drift CI, unit/property/mutation suites, and independently validated evidence bundles are green before close.

comments.text: Blocked preflight only; no claim or source edit while fln-8mj remains in progress. Exact extraction map from the pin: ABI header/tags/layout/calling-convention typedefs and exported LEAN_EXPORT declarations are in vendor/lean4-src/src/include/lean/lean.h; olean v2/v3 header, data section, closure-offset/lib relocation trailer, alignment/base-address rules, and loader checks are in src/library/module.cpp plus src/runtime/compact.{h,cpp}; ModuleData is in src/Lean/Environment.lean; .ilean schema is Lean.Server.Ilean in src/Lean/Server/References.lean and its writer is src/Lean/Elab/Frontend.lean; extern semantics are exposed through Lean.getExternAttrData? in src/Lean/Compiler/ExternAttr.lean. Emit one canonical intermediate inventory into both Markdown and Rust/census outputs so determinism and seeded-drift tests cover every derived artifact.

title: W8: Incremental law — true-cone invalidation, early cutoff, perturbation-validated matrix
description:
Implement the incremental law + invalidation matrix (§13.2): an edit invalidates exactly the records whose hash inputs changed — the TRUE dependency cone computed on recorded edges (franken_networkx dominator/reachability machinery), not the import-file cone. Whitespace, comments, docstrings (non-doc facets), reordering of independent declarations, and edits to unrelated declarations in an imported file invalidate NOTHING downstream. FL-INV-05 (a Ledger hit is semantically identical to recomputation) is enforced by the conservative rule set — anything not proven irrelevant is relevant — plus a standing Tribunal rig that randomly recomputes hits and diffs.

Two refinements make the law sharp: EARLY CUTOFF (a re-run node whose output digest is unchanged stops the cascade — the salsa/Adapton discipline, with statement-hash vs value-hash separation doing the work: an opaque proof-body change invalidates the proof receipt and codegen closure, not its typed dependents); and the PUBLISHED invalidation matrix per change class (trivia-only -> source maps; proof-body edit -> that decl check + receipt + executable closure; type edit -> typed dependents; instance add/remove -> affected class-head search keys via the §10.4 negative-cache dependency keys; notation change -> GrammarEpoch descendants after the activation point; option change -> declared readers; plugin change -> classified reads + ABI load products) — each with its conservative fallback and each PERTURBATION-VALIDATED (mutate an allegedly-irrelevant input, require bit-identical products, demote to opaque on failure). Answer identity vs trace identity is explicit.

Risk R13 (stale hit) law: any confirmed stale hit disables the implicated rule class globally (suffix-barrier fallback) until proven, with a public incident bundle. Acceptance: PG-3 (leaf-edit p50 <= 15 s, p95 <= 90 s warm) trajectory; metamorphic invalidate-nothing suite green; random-recompute rig running. Tests: the C8 edit-sequence corpus with expected cones; perturbation campaigns; clean-rebuild-equivalence.

title: W3: KERNEL_CONTRACT.md — the rule-by-rule Judgment Specification, written first
description:
Write KERNEL_CONTRACT.md — the Judgment Specification (§8.1b, Appendix A) — as the FIRST W3 deliverable, before any engine code: a rule-by-rule account of the implemented type theory, each rule anchored to (a) the Reference source lines that define it and (b) the literature that justifies it. Both kernel engines, the independent checker, and the Tribunal term generators are built against this document.

Inventory (Appendix A): typing rules for every expression constructor; universe-level normalization incl. imax _ 0 = 0; defeq — beta, eta (functions AND structures), delta ordered by definitional height with reducibility-independence, zeta, iota incl. K-like subsingleton elimination, definitional proof irrelevance in Prop, Quot.lift/Quot.mk computation, Nat/String literal rules with the exact accelerated Nat operation set (add, sub, mul, div, mod, pow, gcd, beq, ble, blt, land/lor/lxor/shifts as the pin defines); inductive validation (strict positivity, universe constraints, large elimination, mutual, nested-to-mutual, reflexive); structure eta/projection coherence; declaration admission rules; partial/unsafe quarantine; environment uniqueness rules; resource accounting hooks (counted, never semantic — exhaustion is a verdict about a run, never about a term, FL-INV-07).

The inversion that is itself a product: the Reference specification is its C++; FrankenLean specification is a document the C++ is CHECKED AGAINST, rule by rule, by the differential harness. Acceptance: every rule row has a Reference source anchor, fixtures listed or stubbed, and an unambiguous statement a second implementer could code from; review pass against G0-2 findings; versioned per epoch. Tests (the document is CI-checked like code): an anchor-resolution check verifies every cited Reference source location exists at the pin and fails on drift; a coverage check cross-references the rule inventory against the C0 fixture families (every rule names at least one fixture or an explicit stub with an owner); the Parity Ledger kernel rows link back to rule ids and CI fails on a dangling link; spec-change discipline — any edit to a rule statement requires touching its linked fixtures in the same change or CI flags the divergence, with the diff logged rule-by-rule.

title: Reconcile plan §15 '(fln-trace, fln-graph)' vs §21 crate map (no fln-graph crate)
description:
Plan §15's section title names two crates — fln-trace, fln-graph — but the authoritative cycle-free crate map in §21 (and AGENTS.md's layering chain) lists only fln-trace; the workspace scaffold (bead fln-8mj) follows §21. Either §15's title is stale (Palimpsest's causal-graph store lives inside fln-trace on franken_networkx per §15.3c) and the plan text should drop fln-graph, or a real fln-graph crate is intended and must be added to §21's map + WORKSPACE_GRAPH.txt with a rank and charter. Per the spec-first workflow, amend the plan first, then the workspace. One-line decision; blocks nothing today.

## Acceptance Criteria

The plan is amended before any workspace change. §15 names only the selected owner crate (`fln-trace`); §15.3c still states that Palimpsest stores the causal graph in process using franken_networkx over frankensqlite journals and preserves the optional frankengraphdb export lane; §21, AGENTS.md, ci/WORKSPACE_GRAPH.txt, and the scaffold all agree that no `fln-graph` crate exists. A repository-wide literal search finds no stale `fln-graph` reference. This is a documentation-only architecture decision, so code unit/E2E tests are not applicable; verification is the exact-text search plus cross-document crate-map consistency review recorded in the close reason.

close_reason: Decision: no fln-graph crate. §15.3c already states the causal graph lives in-process on franken_networkx over frankensqlite-backed journals — i.e., inside Palimpsest (fln-trace); §21's authoritative crate map and the fln-8mj scaffold agree. Amended §15's section title to '(fln-trace)' — the single fln-graph reference in the repo (verified by grep). Docs-only decision bead; no code, no workspace change needed. The optional frankengraphdb export remains the frontier lane per §15.3c.

title: W11: Leapfrog surfaces — Bloodhound, Folio, Envoy, terminal InfoView, WASM Judge
description:
Workstream W11 "Leapfrog surfaces" (§16): the products that make FrankenLean more than parity — each strictly downstream of the trust core, each degradable without touching the trust story.

Bloodhound (fln-hound, frankensearch): in-toolchain hybrid lexical+semantic search over the live environment, indexed incrementally at Ledger commit; exact?/apply?-class premise retrieval as a candidate generator whose candidates are VERIFIED by actual elaboration (ranking is UX, proofs are proofs); CandidateCards in three grades (initial/refined/verified); proof repair consuming the causal graph (diff environments, find earliest failed constraint cell, hole the blame slice, search replacements, validate through the full pipeline, emit patch transaction with rollback — the migration autopilot engine); counterfactual analysis and minimization along declared objective vectors. Folio (fln-doc, franken_markdown+fmd-math): doc-gen4-surface-compatible docs as an incremental Ledger facet — native HTML/PDF, native math, receipt-bound pages that go stale honestly rather than lying. Envoy (fln-mcp, fastmcp_rust): the agent door — ProofStateSnapshot as the round-trippable session primitive; budgeted, macaroon-scoped tools (goal_state, apply_tactic, search_lemmas, check_term, eval, get_certificate, ledger_query, trace_query); transactional multi-agent orchestration (patch proposals, never direct trusted mutations; mission receipts with rollback roots). Terminal InfoView (fln goals over frankentui). The WASM Judge (§16.4b): K1 + fln-checker compiled to WASM — sealed-capsule verification in a browser tab, the public face of the trust story.

## Success Criteria

All scope and outcomes stated above remain mandatory, and every dependency/child deliverable required for this epic or gate must be completed at its honest evidence level. The MANDATORY EPIC/GATE CLOSEOUT CONTRACT in Notes must pass with coverage mapping, no-mock E2E evidence, detailed archived human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, reproducible environment facts, and every skip/divergence classified. No feature, compatibility surface, invariant, performance gate, or release claim may be silently narrowed, dropped, or replaced by a prototype.

title: W6: Athanor, Synod & the Mirror — elaborator, instances, scheduler, facade
description:
Workstream W6 "Athanor, Synod & the Native Mirror" (§10, §4.3): the elaborator — the program-defining workstream carrying the two Critical risks (R1 Meta-API dependence, R2 unifier fidelity tail).

Scope: the monadic tower semantics (CoreM/MetaM/TermElabM/TacticM state shapes) as NativeHeap structures with explicit ElabTxn transactions (five outcomes, leak-audited rollback) and an explicit metavariable dependency graph with targeted wake-up; unification/defeq mirroring the Reference approximation ladder EXACTLY, implemented against the instrumented-oracle golden decision traces (§18.3) — the fidelity burden is entirely ours (no hosted fallback exists); term elaboration (coercions, binders, structure instances, match compilation matching generated-name conventions — metaprograms pattern-match on them, they are API; deriving; recursion elaborators lowering to exactly the kernel-visible constructions); Synod instance resolution (Reference semantics — priorities, outParam/semiOutParam, defaults, tabling — behind an indexed relational engine emitting SearchCards + TerminationCards); the NATIVE tactic framework (goal management, evalTactic dispatch, core tactic set behind Mirror facade rows); the deterministic dataflow scheduler (Bet B4: speculative parallel elaboration, canonical-order commit, effect summaries validated by perturbation — FL-INV-01 by construction); the builtin census + generated Mirror facade v1 (census totality: no unclassified symbol, ever — R1 mitigation).

Exit: gate G3 "The Mirror Holds" — Init/Std elaborate end-to-end from source with T2 identity, stdlib double-elaboration fixpoint bit-stable, metaprogram-corpus rig green at declared L-levels, ORACLE_FALLBACK compile-out check passes.

title: W12: fln library crate — the embeddable prover, capability-first API
description:
Implement the fln embeddable library crate (§17.2, the library door): embed the prover — parse, elaborate, check, query — in Rust programs with the SAME engine and the same guarantees as the toolchain binaries; verification inside build tools, CI services, and agents becomes a Cargo.toml line, the posture the Reference architecture makes awkward (process-spawning, stage0, C toolchain) and this one makes trivial.

API design laws: capability-first — an embedder hands the engine a Cx and receives determinism, cancellation, and budget enforcement STRUCTURALLY (no global state, no ambient authority; the embedder controls filesystem/network/clock exposure); typed errors per the D8 taxonomy crossing the API boundary as versioned values, never panics (a panic across the embedding boundary is an invariant failure); the toolchain binaries (fln-cli) are thin shells over this crate so the library IS the tested product, not a parallel implementation; epoch selection explicit (Engine::builder().toolchain_epoch(...)); results carry receipts and logical roots so embedders can persist and re-verify.

Acceptance: an example embedder (checked into examples/) parses, elaborates, and kernel-checks a small project and retrieves a receipt, under a restricted Cx that denies network — demonstrated in CI; fln-cli refactored to consume the library surface; API documented with the semver-per-epoch policy. Tests: embedding e2e test with detailed logging (each API call logged with inputs/outcomes); a panic-containment test (an induced internal fault surfaces as InternalFault, never unwinds across the boundary); Cx-denial tests (denied capabilities produce typed CapabilityDenied); thread-safety smoke under the lab runtime.

It exposes the API that toolchain front doors must consume, but does not claim to refactor nonexistent binaries. franken_lean-eb3 depends on this bead and proves that every CLI personality is a thin shell over the library with a structural no-bypass check.

title: G0-3: Golem execution spike — user-class code + real plugin on ABI-valued VM
description:
G0-3 Golem execution spike (§22.1-3, feeds §11): execute REAL user-class compiled Lean code end-to-end on the prototype VM — an #eval/IO/Task parity micro-corpus compiled from source by the prototype pipeline, plus loading a precompiled Reference-built plugin at the pin — through the prototype intrinsic table operating on ABI values.

The claim being priced: FLBC values ARE Marrow ABI objects, so interpreted code, native intrinsics, and Reference-built plugins share one calling convention with zero conversion. If that identity does not hold in practice (hidden boxing mismatches, RC discipline drift at the call boundary, initializer ordering surprises), the whole membrane design must be amended BEFORE W5/W6 freeze interfaces.

Acceptance: (a) the parity micro-corpus (pure computation, Nat/String/Array ops, closures, IO println/file ops, Task spawn/bind/get ordering, panics, partial non-termination guard) produces observably identical results to the Reference running the same corpus; (b) a real precompiled plugin dlopens, resolves lean_* imports against prototype Marrow exports, runs its initializers in Reference order, and its functions are callable from interpreted code; (c) RC balance verified (no leak, no double-free) under the debug ownership shadow. Tests: the corpus + plugin harness become the seed of the C4 native-ABI probe family and the PG-7 interpreter benchmark baseline.

title: W4: Error recovery + speculative boundaries — repair without changing acceptance
description:
Implement error recovery + the speculative boundary map (§9.3): specified recovery — recovery productions per syntax category, resynchronization tokens, and the RULE that recovery may add diagnostics but never change acceptance. Two boundary products serve the editor: the exact command stream (Reference parser semantics, sole authority over meaning) and a speculative boundary map (a conservative scanner recovering enough structure after malformed edits to schedule work and show syntax diagnostics); speculative boundaries can never commit declarations, and disagreement between the two is journaled as a recovery event.

One law rules everything: no recovered node reaches Athanor without an explicit RecoveredSyntax marker, and no declaration dependent on an unacknowledged repair is ever published (Behavior Note BN-05: extra diagnostics after a syntax error are possible in sound mode; acceptance is unchanged; faithful mode reproduces upstream recovery behavior).

Plus sub-command incremental relex/reparse feeding Lantern snapshots (§14.3): edits inside a command re-lex and re-parse below command granularity where the GrammarEpoch permits. Acceptance: malformed-input corpus keeps serving diagnostics/completions downstream of errors; acceptance-invariance verified (recovery on/off changes no accept/reject verdict on the corpus); incremental reparse products identical to full reparse on the C8 edit sequences. Tests: fuzzed malformed inputs (grammar-mutation fuzzing) never panic and never change acceptance; recovery-event journaling fixtures; RecoveredSyntax-marker propagation tests.

title: G0-6: Fuel-parity spike — calibrate the heartbeat law tick-for-tick
description:
G0-6 Fuel-parity spike (§22.1-6, feeds §4.2/§6.3/§8.5): calibrate the heartbeat-counting law — upstream counts heartbeats in allocation-linked units (maxHeartbeats default 200000, units of thousands) — on an instrumented corpus until faithful-mode timeout behavior matches the Reference tick-for-tick, and document any irreducible divergence class.

Why it matters: determinism of FAILURE is part of determinism. Mathlib and downstream CI depend on maxHeartbeats/maxRecDepth limits firing on the same inputs; a reimplementation that times out differently silently changes which proofs are accepted in practice. The heartbeat hook lives in the owned allocator (§6.3), which is why the allocator is not an off-the-shelf component. This spike also prices risk R11 (heartbeat parity chaotic at fine grain) and resolves OQ-2 (the achievable tolerance class).

Acceptance: (a) an instrumented corpus where Reference heartbeat consumption is captured per declaration (instrumented-oracle apparatus); (b) prototype counting law reproduces timeout/no-timeout verdicts on every corpus entry, with tick deltas quantified; (c) a declared tolerance class (exact / bounded-drift / law-level parity per BN-08) ratified into the plan; (d) maxRecDepth accounting verified on deep-recursion fixtures. Tests: fuel-parity fixtures join the faithful-mode differential rig; any future allocator change must keep them green.

title: W11: Envoy — MCP tools, ProofStateSnapshot, macaroon authority, multi-agent orchestration
description:
Implement Envoy (fln-mcp, §16.3): the agent door — MCP tools over the Lantern session API: goal_state, apply_tactic (against forked snapshots, budgeted), search_lemmas (Bloodhound), check_term, eval (capability-scoped), get_certificate, ledger_query, trace_query (Palimpsest). Budgets (heartbeats, wall, memory, expanded weight) are first-class parameters; every tool result carries provenance; read tools are sound-mode; write-to-file tools are explicit and default-off.

The session primitive: ProofStateSnapshot — a versioned, schema-registered, canonical serialization of an elaboration point (goals with local contexts, metavariable assignments, environment logical root, journal position, option set) — ROUND-TRIPPABLE (a snapshot IS a resumable state), stable across releases, structurally shared with the live store. Authority is macaroon-scoped (asupersync tokens): a credential names readable environment roots, the budget envelope, the consensus policy it may request, and whether write-tools exist — attenuable delegation, every check receipted. Robot responses carry schema/epoch/profile versions, request + snapshot ids, resource facts, data grade (provisional/verified), evidence links; mutating tools carry idempotency keys + preconditions.

On top: transactional multi-agent orchestration — fork a branch per agent, assign disjoint impact regions from Palimpsest cones, stream scoped context packs, accept PATCH PROPOSALS (never direct trusted mutations), re-elaborate + kernel-check each, detect semantic conflicts via §15.3b, merge deterministically, emit a mission receipt with a rollback root — agent success measured by accepted kernel-checked commits. Plus the remote check service (sealed capsule delta in, full-policy consensus verdict + signed transparency-logged receipt out; workers may lie, results validate locally). Acceptance: the tool surface validated against a frozen JSON schema contract; snapshot round-trips bit-stable; a two-agent orchestration scenario end-to-end with a semantic-conflict block demonstrated. Tests: budget-exhaustion typed results (FL-INV-07 over the wire); macaroon attenuation fixtures (a narrowed token cannot widen); idempotency/precondition replay tests; schema-freeze contract tests.

title: W2: stage0 ABI gauntlet — upstream generated C runs the runtime suite against Marrow
description:
Stand up the stage0 ABI gauntlet as a standing Tribunal lane (§6.6, §18.2): compile the Reference own stage0-generated C (test apparatus only, via the optional D2 cc) against Marrow exported lean_* surface, and run the upstream runtime test suite through the membrane.

Why this is the strongest ABI rig available: stage0 is the exact code the ecosystem ships — millions of lines of generated C exercising every corner of the object model, RC discipline, boxing rules, and exported function census. If the membrane is wrong ANYWHERE, upstream own code says so, loudly and early, with a concrete failing call site. This displaces guesswork about ABI corner cases with an executable oracle. Note the D8 boundary: stage0 here is TEST APPARATUS (fixture corpus), never a runtime component; the lane runs in the Tribunal sandbox and its products are conformance evidence only.

Acceptance: the gauntlet builds and links against Marrow on linux-x86-64 first (other platforms staged); the upstream runtime suite passes on the implemented ABI subset with every failure triaged to a contract row (fix or honest Unsupported status); the lane runs in CI on membrane-touching changes. Tests: this IS the test; additionally a seeded-defect mutation (perturb one exported function ownership convention — the gauntlet must catch it, proving the lane kills mutants per §18.2).

title: W11: Folio — native docs facet, receipt-bound pages, incremental rendering
description:
Implement Folio (fln-doc, §16.2): doc-gen4-surface-compatible documentation as a Ledger facet — docstring markdown through franken_markdown, mathematics through fmd-math rendered natively to HTML and PDF (zero browser-side math stack), syntax highlighting via a Quill-fed lexer, cross-reference and instance graphs via franken_networkx, INCREMENTAL (only changed declarations re-render — mathlib docs become minutes, local, offline).

Documents are receipt-bound: a rendered page embeds the logical root and receipt links for every checked excerpt, and a later source change marks the artifact STALE rather than leaving a verified badge lying (the Witness discipline of §20.4 applied to documentation). The frontier lane: franken_manim consumes Palimpsest event sequences to render animated proof explanations (goal transformations under a tactic, unification constraints resolving, induction structure, before/after of a repair) — explanatory projections that link to checked declarations and are never themselves certificates.

Acceptance: doc output for a mathlib slice matches the doc-gen4 surface conventions (URLs, anchors, structure) so existing links and tooling survive; incremental re-render touches only the changed-declaration cone; receipt-staleness demonstrated (edit a source, the page marks stale). Tests: rendering golden tests (HTML structure + PDF layout smoke); math-rendering fixtures vs known TeX corpus; incremental-facet metamorphic tests (doc-irrelevant edits re-render nothing); link-integrity checks across the generated set.

title: W8: Ledger & Lake — content-addressed build fabric, invalidation law, cache
description:
Workstream W8 "Ledger & Lake" (§13): the build fabric (Bet B1) — the environment as a Merkle DAG of content-addressed declarations; builds as memoized queries; the ecosystem quality-of-life complaint #1 (file-granular invalidation) deleted.

Scope: Ledger records (decl_hash over name, elaborated statement, value class, direct dep hashes, options-that-matter, epoch, mode) generalized to demand nodes across five planes (source/language/trust/build/service) with retention tiers T0-T4; the incremental law (§13.2): true-dependency-cone invalidation on recorded edges, early cutoff (unchanged output digest stops the cascade; statement-hash vs value-hash separation), and the published, fixture-tested, perturbation-validated invalidation matrix per change class with conservative fallbacks (FL-INV-05: anything not proven irrelevant is relevant); the Lake surface (lakefile.lean executes on Golem — the config DSL is user code, so Golem IS the compatibility mechanism; lakefile.toml fast path; require via D2 git; exit codes and --json pinned; fln build explain shows Reference-decision vs native-decision); cache federation (CAS sync over atp multi-donor fountain-coded with HTTPS fallback; remote entries are untrusted until validated — malformed entry = miss + security event; fln cache shim preserves community muscle memory); epoch-stable caching + bridges (frontier, allowlisted, separately gated) + the migration autopilot (§13.6); watch mode + distributed elaboration (results re-verified by local Crucible — distribution without distributed trust).

Exit feeds gates G4 (mixed-build matrix) and G6 (PG-3 cone, PG-10 cache).

## Success Criteria

All scope and outcomes stated above remain mandatory, and every dependency/child deliverable required for this epic or gate must be completed at its honest evidence level. The MANDATORY EPIC/GATE CLOSEOUT CONTRACT in Notes must pass with coverage mapping, no-mock E2E evidence, detailed archived human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, reproducible environment facts, and every skip/divergence classified. No feature, compatibility surface, invariant, performance gate, or release claim may be silently narrowed, dropped, or replaced by a prototype.
design: REVIEW AMENDMENT — W8 closure includes fln-publish-rewrite-indexes-gca so the plan's "CAS-shipped per-library indexes" claim is proven at the subsystem boundary, not implied by a standalone W7 serializer. The migration autopilot itself is downstream integration in franken_lean-epoch-migration-autopilot-q9w; W8 provides sound cache/bridge/invalidation primitives without a placeholder repair engine.
notes:
MANDATORY EPIC/GATE CLOSEOUT CONTRACT:
This epic/gate may close only when every child deliverable is complete; the verification registry maps every implemented public behavior, FL-INV claim, Parity-Ledger row, Behavior Note, and PG/gate claim to named unit/property/mutation coverage plus a shared no-mock E2E scenario; detailed human + schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts are archived; all divergences and skips are classified; no mock-only evidence is used for a release claim; and the final evidence bundle records exact reproduction commands, environment/epoch/mode/platform/thread/seed/cache facts, input hashes, receipts/logical roots, expected final states, first divergences, and cleanup results.

title: W4: GrammarEpoch + memoization — grammar as versioned data, typed parser effects
description:
Implement GrammarEpoch versioning + parse memoization (§9.1): the grammar state as versioned DATA, not hidden globals — a GrammarEpoch content-hashes the active syntax declarations, parser descriptors, precedence tables, scoped notation, token parsers, and relevant options; every parse node binds the epoch it was recognized under; memoization keys on (position, category, precedence, GrammarEpoch); a syntax-changing command invalidates exactly the parse products that read the changed components.

Parser registrations emit typed effect summaries (AddsSyntax, AddsToken, ChangesPrecedence, OpensScope, RegistersMacro, OpaqueParserEffect) — an opaque effect is legal for fidelity but forces the conservative suffix barrier and disables distributed parsing past that point (precision is earned by evidence, never assumed; the system starts conservative and ratchets, §10.6).

Why load-bearing: this is what makes sub-command incremental reparse (Lantern), the Ledger notation/syntax invalidation-matrix row, and distributed parsing sound rather than hopeful. Acceptance: epoch hashing deterministic and stable across hosts; memoization hit/miss behavior verified against the invalidation matrix fixtures (a notation change invalidates exactly the affected epoch descendants after the activation point); opaque-effect barrier demonstrated. Tests: property tests — memoized parse == fresh parse under any epoch-respecting edit sequence; C8 incremental edit-sequence corpus with expected invalidation cones.

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.

title: W3: Crucible & Bignum — dual-engine kernel, independent checker, receipts
description:
Workstream W3 "Crucible & Bignum" (§8): the trusted kernel — the one subsystem where FrankenLean volunteers to be outnumbered — plus the owned arithmetic beneath it.

Deliverable order is constitutional: KERNEL_CONTRACT.md (the Judgment Specification, §8.1b) comes FIRST — a rule-by-rule account of the implemented type theory, each rule anchored to Reference source lines, against which both engines and the Tribunal generators are built. Then: K1 certified small-step evaluator (auditability-first, the code a skeptical logician reads in an afternoon); K2 NbE accelerator (memoized whnf keyed on term-hash x transparency, lazy delta by height) cross-checked against K1 continuously; fln-checker, a deliberately DIFFERENT independent implementation (eager normalization, own decoder over canonical wire objects, no unsafe-membrane dependency, WASM-clean, non-sharing proven structurally); fln-bignum (limb-based, ABI-layout-matching, thresholds tuned on the kernel-reduction corpus, PG-K gate vs GMP); typestate admission (UnvalidatedExpr -> ScopedExpr -> CheckedExpr, DeclarationEnvelope, four-way verdict incl. Inconclusive per FL-INV-07); typed resource budgets (§8.2c); fuel parity (§8.5); certificates + receipts + transparency log (§8.6); consensus policies where disagreement halts and never outvotes (§8.3c).

Covenant: fln-kernel <= 12 KLOC, forbid(unsafe), deps = fln-bignum + read-only term views + core/alloc only, one exported authority (check), CI-counted. Divergence law: kernel disagreement with the Reference at the pin is release-blocking; soundness beats bug-parity (D23). Exit: gate G1 — every mathlib olean re-checked with zero acceptance divergence, PG-1 met.

## Success Criteria

title: Gate G3: The Mirror Holds — Init/Std from source, T2 identity, fixpoint bit-stable
description:
Convergence gate G3 "The Mirror Holds" (§22.3): the native toolchain elaborates Init and Std end-to-end FROM SOURCE — every builtin either native behind its facade row or library-code source-elaborated, zero upstream implementation executing anywhere — with T2 identity against the Reference, the stdlib double-elaboration fixpoint bit-stable, and the metaprogram-corpus rig (curated mathlib-tactic slice through the facade) green at its declared L-levels. The ORACLE_FALLBACK compile-out check passes on the gate build.

This is the gate that proves the central bet (B2): the parser, elaborator, instance engine, tactic framework, and scheduler reproduce the Reference at stdlib scale, and real ecosystem metaprogram code cannot distinguish the native facade from the machinery it replaced. It is the last gate before the full Corpus becomes the target.

Evidence bundle: T2 differential results over Init/Std (acceptance, diagnostics, statement-level environment identity); the fixpoint runs (bit-identical oleans + logical roots across double elaboration and a freshly rebuilt toolchain); metaprogram-rig results with the facade-row L-level table; census-totality check output; the D8 compile-out proof on the gate binaries; PG-5 thread-matrix results at stdlib scale.

title: Gate G4: The Corpus Whole — full mathlib, T2=100%, PG-2, mixed builds
description:
Convergence gate G4 "The Corpus Whole" (§22.3): full mathlib elaboration — T2 = 100%, T3 measured with a published ratchet, PG-2(G4) met (wall <= 0.5x Reference, same machine/threads), olean outputs consumed by the Reference toolchain in the mixed-build matrix. This is the gate that makes "an implementation of Lean" a fact rather than a claim: the Corpus is the de-facto behavioral specification (§1.9), and elaborating all of it with statement-identical environments is the definition of having implemented the language.

Also folded in per §22.2/§12.6: grind rows at L3 on the grind corpus; simp fidelity at scale; the Ledger incremental law live under the full Corpus (the PG-3 measurement baseline); native_decide Tier 1 as the sound-mode default; PG-M (metaprogram corpus <= 1.25x Reference).

Evidence bundle: the full T2/T3 differential ledger with every T3 residual classified (fidelity defect closed, or documented free choice with pinned CGSE policy); PG-2/PG-M numbers with claim states; the mixed-producer matrix transcripts (our oleans under the Reference, theirs under ours, interleaved builds); the R2 divergence ledger empty or published as errata with quantified blast radius (the epoch ships R3, not R4, until closed).

title: W12: Front doors — lean/leanc/lake personalities + the fln multiplexer verbs
description:
Implement the toolchain front doors (§17.1, fln-cli): the binaries lean, leanc (the C-driver personality: flag-compatible, driving the D2 cc when present, explaining its absence via capability errors when not), lake, and the fln multiplexer exposing the new verbs — check-olean, audit (--tcb), replay, doctor (--sql), cache, olean, goals, serve-mcp, why-trusts, diff, build explain, server replay, verify-capsule, identity.

Contract: flags, exit codes, env vars (LEAN_PATH, LEAN_SYSROOT), and --json shapes pinned to the Reference at the pin (CLI transcript corpus, C-family fixtures); --trust levels ACCEPTED with upstream semantics but journaled into receipts and surfaced by fln audit rather than silently honored (BN-09); new flags live under --fln-* only; the version line stays parse-compatible (Reference version/commit fields preserved) with a separate machine-readable implementation field — compatibility never becomes deception, and an exact-reference transcript mode exists for conformance harnesses only. Robot-mode discipline per AGENTS.md: stable versioned schemas, deterministic where possible, explicit exit codes, line-oriented output, no human decoration mixed into machine streams.

Acceptance: the CLI transcript corpus diffs clean against the Reference for the shared surface; each fln verb has a --json schema contract test against a frozen fixture; elan invokes the binaries transparently via the release layout (verified in W12). Tests: golden transcripts per verb (human and JSON modes) with detailed logging of any drift; exit-code matrix fixtures; --trust journaling fixtures; capability-error fixtures for missing cc/git naming the alternative.

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. design: REVIEW AMENDMENT — this is the final front-door integration, not an early flag parser. Every advertised verb must route to its real owning subsystem and the fln library API: check-olean, audit/why-trusts/diff/replay/doctor, Lake/build-explain, cache, olean/capsules, goals, serve-mcp, server replay, and identity/Witness facts. A structural test rejects direct engine bypasses from CLI crates. Shared Reference-compatible personalities and new robot schemas retain distinct transcript fixtures and detailed E2E logs.

title: W7: Engine-backed tactic facade integration — simp, norm_num, omega, bv_decide, grind
description:
Complete the engine-backed core-tactic surface after the W6 native tactic framework and W7 engines exist. Wire the stable facade/dispatch contracts to the faithful simp core, norm_num and omega certificate producers, Verdict bv_decide, and native grind; execute both direct core invocations and real Golem-hosted user tactics through CoreM/MetaM/TermElabM/TacticM; require every produced declaration to pass Crucible and the configured consensus policy (FL-INV-02/06).

This bead removes a stage-order contradiction: W6 owns goal/state/backtracking/InfoTree semantics and the bootstrap-safe primitive tactic set; W7 owns these engine-backed rows. Until this bead lands, the corresponding census rows remain at their honest L0/L1 state and return precise typed Unsupported results where invoked — no fake implementation, compatibility shim, or hidden oracle.

Acceptance: the metaprogram corpus exercises each engine-backed row through the production facade; environments, InfoTrees, diagnostics, generated names, tactic state/backtracking, and goal-closure behavior match the Reference at each row's declared comparison class; simp/grind decision traces replay; norm_num/omega/Verdict certificates replay through Crucible; unsupported markers for the completed rows disappear only when their evidence rows promote.

Verification: per-row unit and negative tests, backtracking/failure-atomicity properties, bogus-certificate and direct-admission mutation tests, budget/cancellation scenarios, and shared no-mock E2E scripts running real tactic files on Golem with detailed step, trace, receipt, and first-divergence logging.
