title: W12: Epoch migration autopilot integration — audited repair, explicit apply, rollback
description:
Integrate the §13.6 migration autopilot at the stage where all of its real prerequisites exist: the W12 epoch ratchet and machine-diffed censuses, W8 epoch bridges/cache identities, W9 semantic diff and blame slices, and W11 Bloodhound's verified proof-repair engine. Audit an unchanged downstream project against the new epoch; locate the earliest failed constraints and affected surfaces; propose minimal source/config/import/termination/instance/simp repairs; run lockstep old/new builds; validate every candidate through Athanor, Crucible, and the configured consensus policy; and emit an explicit apply transaction with a sealed rollback bundle. It never edits a project without an explicit apply.

Issue-tracker integration must respect D2: shipped FrankenLean tooling never spawns br or any third external tool. The ratchet emits a deterministic, schema-versioned migration-issue manifest with one row per moved symbol/divergence and exact br import/apply instructions for the repository-maintenance harness or human operator; applying that manifest is outside release-binary execution and is separately receipted.

Acceptance: a synthetic epoch delta and at least one real historical Lean migration run end to end; unchanged projects that need no repair remain byte/semantic clean; repaired projects build under the new epoch with every patch kernel-checked; rejected or partially applied migrations restore exactly from the rollback root; unclassified divergences block publication; migration issue manifests are deterministic and complete.

Verification: unit tests for census-to-impact mapping and transaction preconditions; property tests for rollback identity/idempotent re-apply; repair-ranking and explicit-consent negatives; lockstep divergence fixtures; and shared no-mock E2E scripts covering audit -> propose -> verify -> preview -> explicit apply -> rollback, with detailed old/new roots, patch hashes, receipts, issue-manifest rows, commands, final states, and first-divergence logs.

title: W6: Builtin census + Mirror facade — census-total, evidence-leveled, no hidden oracle
description:
Implement the builtin census + generated Mirror facade v1 (§4.3, Appendix C): mechanical extraction over the pinned Init/Std/Lean sources emitting the census (symbol, Lean signature, universe/binder telescope, module, kind, attributes, extern symbol, effect class, PARTITION CLASS: toolchain-api / library-code / user-facing-data) and, from the toolchain-api subset, generated facade stubs (upstream signature, telescope, effect class) whose bodies call the native Quill/Athanor/Crucible/Golem/Anvil/Marrow services.

The three laws: (1) census TOTALITY — no unclassified symbol; a symbol the census cannot classify blocks the surface L-level, never gets guessed. (2) Fidelity by evidence (FL-INV-03) — every toolchain-api row proven against the oracle: fixture differentials, golden decision traces, the metaprogram-corpus rig; a row whose API intrinsically exposes internal object identity or allocation order is classified at its honest L-level with a Behavior Note, never hand-waved. (3) No hidden oracle (D8) — the fallback for a facade defect is fixing the native implementation against fixtures; there is no run-the-upstream-definition switch in any release binary. Every row states: symbol, upstream semantics anchor (source location at the pin), equivalence class (bit/obs/perf), evidence, determinism class, L-level.

Acceptance: G3 requirement — Init/Std elaborate end-to-end from source with every builtin either native-behind-its-row or library-code source-elaborated, zero upstream implementation executing; census diff tooling ready for epoch advances; the G0-8 resistance list resolved row by row. Tests: census-totality CI check; facade-stub compile checks against real metaprogram code; per-row differential fixture families.

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.

title: W2: Marrow & Grimoire — ABI twin, object model, environment, olean codec
description:
Workstream W2 "Marrow & Grimoire" (§6, §7): the runtime/ABI twin and the environment/module codec — the load-bearing act of drop-in compatibility and the spine of gate G1.

Marrow: the Reference object model implemented exactly in Rust under the lean_* exported names (header packing, tag bytes, scalar-field packing, odd-tagged-pointer small Nats, tri-state RC discipline), with layout constants compiled from the GENERATED contract tables of §6.5 — never transcribed. Two heaps, one membrane (§6.1b): CompatHeap (exact ABI layouts, membrane-only allocation, unsafe confined to fln-unsafe-abi) vs NativeHeap (typed asupersync region handles for toolchain-internal structures); values cross only through generated converters; no raw Compat pointer ever enters the kernel (D22). Owned size-classed allocator with the heartbeat hook (fuel parity lives here). Compacted regions: construction, mmap-load, relocation — deliberately the SAME code path the olean codec uses.

Grimoire: the environment as persistent HAMT-style maps with O(1) snapshots and logical roots (§7.1); total contractual interning with alpha-canonical digest lane (§7.1b); olean codec read (three validation levels) and faithful byte-identical write (FL-INV-04); .ilean codec; olean-next frontier lane; import-at-daemon-scale shared heap (§7.5). Effects ride asupersync behind Reference IO/Task semantics (§6.7). fln-libm owned deterministic transcendentals (§6.8). Exit: G1 spine ready — real mathlib oleans resurrect, re-emit byte-identically, and load into the Reference.

## Success Criteria

All scope and outcomes stated above remain mandatory, and every dependency/child deliverable required for this epic or gate must be completed at its honest evidence level. The MANDATORY EPIC/GATE CLOSEOUT CONTRACT in Notes must pass with coverage mapping, no-mock E2E evidence, detailed archived human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, reproducible environment facts, and every skip/divergence classified. No feature, compatibility surface, invariant, performance gate, or release claim may be silently narrowed, dropped, or replaced by a prototype.
notes:
MANDATORY EPIC/GATE CLOSEOUT CONTRACT:
This epic/gate may close only when every child deliverable is complete; the verification registry maps every implemented public behavior, FL-INV claim, Parity-Ledger row, Behavior Note, and PG/gate claim to named unit/property/mutation coverage plus a shared no-mock E2E scenario; detailed human + schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts are archived; all divergences and skips are classified; no mock-only evidence is used for a release claim; and the final evidence bundle records exact reproduction commands, environment/epoch/mode/platform/thread/seed/cache facts, input hashes, receipts/logical roots, expected final states, first divergences, and cleanup results.

title: W6: Deterministic dataflow scheduler — speculative parallelism, canonical merge, perturbation-validated effects
description:
Implement the deterministic dataflow scheduler (Bet B4, §10.6): declarations as dataflow nodes — parse yields a conservative dependency pre-scan; elaboration proceeds speculatively in parallel asupersync regions against O(1) environment snapshots; a declaration that reads a not-yet-committed name suspends on its true dependency or aborts-and-retries; commits merge in CANONICAL ORDER (source order per module; module order per Ledger topology) so the resulting environment, diagnostics, and InfoTrees are those of sequential elaboration — bit-for-bit, at any thread count (FL-INV-01 by construction, the invariant upstream Elab.async demonstrably lacks).

Mechanics: optimistic concurrency — transactions get source-order IDs; commit-time read-set validation against the latest committed predecessor either commits in canonical order or discards/rebases; no last-writer-wins anywhere; conflicting extension mutations serialize. Side-effecting commands are ordering barriers detected conservatively: unknown effects serialize; known-commutative registered effects parallelize. Command effect summaries (ReadsDecl(name, Type|Value|Attributes), ReadsInstances(head), ReadsSimpSet, ReadsGrammar, ReadsOption, WritesDecl, WritesInstance, WritesGrammar, WritesEnvExtension, UsesCapability, Opaque(reason)) are captured dynamically and validated BY PERTURBATION: mutate an allegedly-unread input, require the product unchanged; a failing perturbation demotes the producer to Opaque and opens a Parity-Ledger bead. Cross-module speculation rides Ledger hashes-in-flight; mis-speculation costs a retry, never a wrong answer.

Acceptance: PG-5 thread-matrix {1,8,32} bit-identical environments/diagnostics/artifacts per commit on the corpus; speculation win measured against the sequential baseline; perturbation validator running in CI. Tests: DPOR-bounded schedule exploration of the merge protocol under the asupersync lab; abort/retry storm fixtures; seeded effect-summary lies must be demoted.

title: G0-9: Instrumented-oracle spike — golden decision traces schema, volume, replay rig
description:
G0-9 Instrumented-oracle spike (§22.1-9, feeds §18.3): patch the Reference IN THE TRIBUNAL SANDBOX (build-time-only, test-only, never shipped, never linked — D8) to dump decision traces at the points our native machinery must match — unifier approximation choices, postponements, unfoldings and their order, instance-search candidate orders and outcomes, macro expansions, simp rewrite firings, heartbeat consumption — on a pilot corpus, and prove the trace schema, volume, and replay-rig design.

Why this exists: under the Oracle-Only Law there is NO hosted upstream engine to fall back to, so the unifier/instance/simp fidelity burden (Critical risk R2) is carried entirely by evidence. Golden decision traces at Corpus scale are the strongest available mitigation: Athanor, Synod, and Anvil are implemented AGAINST the traces, not against vibes, and trace-replay rigs run continuously thereafter.

Acceptance: (a) the patched-oracle build recipe is scripted, hashed, and reproducible in the sandbox; (b) trace schema versioned and validated (typed events, source anchors, stable ids); (c) volume measured on the pilot corpus with a stated projection to Corpus scale (storage in the Tribunal frankensqlite store); (d) a replay rig prototype diffs a hand-written toy unifier against traces to prove the comparison machinery. Traces are fixtures, never components; the generator lives behind the D8 wall. Tests: the pilot traces + replay rig land in fln-conformance as the seed of the continuous golden-trace lanes.

title: W3: Crucible K2 — NbE accelerator, memoized whnf, cross-checked against K1
description:
Implement Crucible engine K2, the NbE accelerator (§8.3): normalization-by-evaluation with environment-aware closures, memoized whnf keyed on (term-hash, transparency), lazy delta with definitional-height comparison — the engine that makes decide, big Fin computations, and mathlib defeq-heavy corners fast (the PG-1/PG-2 arithmetic depends on it; the design was priced by spike G0-7).

Laws: K2 implements the SAME judgments as K1 (one KERNEL_CONTRACT); K2 verdicts are spot-audited against K1 continuously (sampled per run, exhaustively in Tribunal soaks) and any disagreement fails loudly and ships as a fixture; every fast-lane shortcut carries exactly one registered justification — a local proof argument in the kernel design note, a certificate checked by a simpler routine, or differential evidence (which licenses an optimization and never impersonates a proof). The size covenant counts K2 lines too. Cache keying uses the alpha-canonical digest lane (§7.1b) so alpha-varied duplicates share reduction work invisibly to metaprograms.

Acceptance: the G0-7 twenty-problem tail suite meets the envelope the PG-2 model requires; K1/K2 continuous cross-check wired and green on the full fixture corpus; PG-1 trajectory measured (full mathlib olean recheck time on the 32-core profile). Tests: differential K1-vs-K2 on generated term storms; memoization-correctness property tests (cached verdict == fresh verdict under transparency changes); fuel-parity fixtures shared with G0-6 stay green on both engines.

title: W3: fln-checker — structurally independent checker, WASM-clean, consensus seat
description:
Implement fln-checker, the independent kernel checker (§8.3b): a deliberately DIFFERENT second implementation, not a feature flag over fln-kernel — separate normalization algorithm (eager where K1/K2 are lazy), simpler persistent maps and substitutions, its OWN decoder over Grimoire canonical wire objects (never the primary heap nodes), no dependency on the unsafe membrane, an independently derived error-class mapping, a separate build artifact, and a WASM build for a foreign execution substrate (the WASM Judge second engine).

Non-sharing is proven STRUCTURALLY: CI verifies fln-checker shares data schemas but no semantic implementation modules with fln-kernel (dependency-graph rules). The checker joins the consensus policies of §8.3c: standard (deterministic content-seeded sample + all high-risk forms — never runtime randomness), release (full closure), paranoid (everywhere, optionally distinct hosts). The honest economics: roughly 2x kernel CPU where it runs, single-digit percent end-to-end (PG-1b measures it). Common-mode risk R6 (shared Judgment Specification misreading) is why FOREIGN witnesses (lean4checker, lean4lean via export) exist in the council; this bead includes wiring the external-witness sampling seam.

Acceptance: full agreement with K1/K2 on the corpus; a seeded spec-divergence mutant (deliberately misimplement one rule in a test build) is caught by the differential lane; WASM build passes the same vectors bit-for-bit. Tests: non-sharing CI check; consensus-policy integration tests where a planted disagreement HALTS publication with both traces attached (never outvoted).

title: W5: Golem runtime integration — generated intrinsics, effects, plugins, G2 parity
description:
Integrate the production Golem execution stack after its independently testable components exist: validated FIR-to-FLBC output, the FLBC interpreter host-call ABI, the census-generated intrinsic table, Marrow allocation/RC, asupersync IO/Task effects, initializer ordering and capability receipts, and the inbound Reference-plugin loader. This bead owns the no-mock end-to-end proof of the §11 claim; component beads may prove their own stable interfaces without pretending that future components already exist.

Required behavior: compile and execute real user-class Lean code (mathlib-style tactics, project macros, lakefile-shaped configuration, #eval and IO/Task bodies) on ABI-valued registers; cross interpreted code <-> native intrinsics <-> Reference-built plugin calls with zero representation conversion; preserve initialization/extension ordering; count heartbeats through the same allocator hook; enforce typed budgets and cancellation; retain RC balance and ownership-shadow evidence; record capability and plugin provenance. No test stub, prototype intrinsic, or oracle artifact may satisfy production acceptance.

Acceptance: the G0-3 corpus is promoted to the complete production stack; #eval/IO/Task and plugin results match the Reference at each registered comparison class; PG-7 interpreter measurements are emitted against the W1 baseline; the C4 inbound matrix is green at honest per-platform L-levels; initialization-DAG and capability receipts are complete; two runs at each certified thread count produce identical logical results and evidence identities where required.

Verification: unit contract tests at every component seam plus shared no-mock E2E scripts for pure computation, IO/Task ordering, capability denial, cancellation, panic/partial guards, plugin load/run/crash, and RC/heartbeat accounting. Every scenario emits the mandatory human and NDJSON evidence with the first cross-boundary divergence and final ownership/obligation state.

title: W5: Initialization + capability-scoped effects — ordering, --pure-elab, receipts
description:
Implement initialization + capability-scoped effects (§11.5): initialize / builtin_initialize ordering, import-time extension replay interleaving, and real-world IO effects exactly as upstream — under asupersync capabilities, so a --pure-elab audit mode can DENY ambient filesystem/network/clock to metaprograms and report exactly which declarations demanded them.

The posture: capability-scoped metaprogramming is a sound-mode observability win and a frontier-mode sandbox, NEVER a semantic change to compliant code (faithful mode default ambient authority approximates an ordinary Lean process — drop-in means unchanged packages just run, with effects OBSERVED into provenance). Initialization DAG receipts (from the plugin door, §6.6) unify with this: cycles, duplicate initializers, and late capability requests are explainable. This bead also covers the interaction with Golem-hosted lakefile execution (Ledger, §13.3) — lakefiles are user code with declared effect surfaces.

Acceptance: initializer-ordering fixtures match Reference-observable behavior incl. interleaving with extension replay; --pure-elab on a corpus with known effectful metaprograms reports exactly the offender set; capability events land in Palimpsest with correct spans. Tests: ordering property tests over generated init DAGs; denial fixtures (a denied capability yields typed CapabilityDenied naming the alternative, never silent substitution — D2 discipline); provenance-completeness checks against the perturbation validator.

title: G0-4: Hygiene & syntax fidelity spike — macro-heavy corpus tree/position diff
description:
G0-4 Hygiene & syntax fidelity spike (§22.1-4, feeds §9): parse a curated set of macro-heavy Corpus files and compare Syntax trees, byte positions, and hygiene observables against the Reference (driven through its own frontend under instrumentation in the Tribunal sandbox).

Hygiene is the classic reimplementation graveyard: macro scopes appended to names, quotation/antiquotation semantics, macro_rules matching, and the parse/elaborate interleaving (a command can install a parser used three lines later) are all API surface that metaprograms and diagnostics observe. This spike visits the graveyard early. Curate files whose MEANING depends on hygiene corner cases: scope-shadowing across macro expansion, antiquotation splices, syntax categories with custom precedence, notation-priority interactions, auto-bound implicit capture.

Acceptance: (a) node kinds, atom/ident structure, SourceInfo (original vs synthetic, whitespace attachment, UTF-8 byte positions) byte-agree on the curated set; (b) hygiene-decorated generated names agree under the stable-expansion-path derivation; (c) every divergence triaged: fidelity defect (fix) or undocumented-upstream-behavior (fixture + upstream report + Behavior Note candidate); (d) findings feed the fln-syntax / fln-parse interface freeze. Tests: the curated set becomes the standing hygiene fidelity rig in fln-conformance, run per-commit.

title: W10: Backends & ABI doors — Iron native backend, JIT, C parity, outbound plugins
description:
Workstream W10 "Backends & ABI doors" (§17.3, §17.4, §11.3): the sovereign compilation path and the bidirectional plugin promise.

Scope: Iron static backend — FLBC to native objects/executables (x86-64, aarch64 first) with NO LLVM, no cranelift, no cc: the owned baseline emitter grown a static mode; lake build of executables with zero external toolchain (the G6 headline). Iron-JIT (frontier, standard-mode only, fln-unsafe-jit): baseline template JIT with inline caches burned in; results bit-identical to the interpreter by construction, spot-checked by a standing dual-run rig; certified builds never enable it. --backend c parity generator: emit C in the Reference idiom and drive the USER toolchain exactly as upstream leanc does (D2 optional tool #1) — parity apparatus and exotic-target escape hatch, never the anchor. Both backends dual-run governed: Iron pinned to interpreter results, interpreter pinned to Reference observable evaluation semantics on the differential corpus. ABI door outbound (§17.4, the harder direction, staged to G5): FrankenLean-built plugins loading into the Reference host, per-platform Tribunal matrix; inbound (Reference plugins into FrankenLean) lands earlier under W2/G2. Risk R5 honesty: outbound tiers per platform in the Parity Ledger rather than blocking G5.

title: W2: olean-next + fln olean tool — content-addressed modules, sealed capsules, diffs
description:
Implement olean-next + the fln olean tool (§7.4, frontier): the second module lane — a content-addressed, position-independent format: per-declaration records keyed by Ledger hashes, structure-shared term DAGs with dictionary compression, mergeable and diffable, mmap-friendly WITHOUT relocation, carrying certificate attachments (§8.6) inline. olean-next never replaces olean in faithful/sound artifacts (D-08); it is the Ledger native interchange and the format proof-cache federation speaks.

Capsule shapes: THIN (references into the shared CAS) and SEALED (a complete transitive object pack, verifiable on an air-gapped machine — the archival/audit form, and the .flnpack the WASM Judge consumes). Attested evidence objects may carry RaptorQ repair sidecars via the asupersync coder; repair reconstructs to staging, re-verifies the canonical hash and a full schema decode, publishes without changing identity, and journals the corruption/repair event — repair is a durability feature, NEVER a trust shortcut. The fln olean CLI tool converts both directions (olean <-> olean-next) and prints human-readable diffs of module contents — the un-opaquing of weakness census #8 (pointer-dump oleans are unmergeable, un-diffable, un-addressable).

Acceptance: round-trip olean -> olean-next -> olean preserves semantic hash and (faithful path) bytes; sealed capsules verify air-gapped (no CAS access); repair-sidecar loss and corruption drills pass with journaled events; diff output validated on known module pairs. Tests: format fuzzing under budgets; thin/sealed equivalence property tests; a tamper test (any flipped byte fails verification with a typed error naming the object); dictionary-compression determinism (same input, same bytes).

title: W12: Epoch ratchet — mechanical pin advances, census diffs, migration beads
description:
Implement the epoch-advance ratchet as tooling (§22.5, §13.6): the mechanical 8 steps — (1) fetch + hash the official release into the epoch lab; (2) regenerate every census on every platform (ABI, olean, extern, builtin, options, CLI, LSP schemas); (3) run the existing corpus differentially and mine new behavior into fixtures + fresh instrumented-oracle traces; (4) machine-diff the public/Meta API, artifact, ABI, CLI, Lake, LSP surfaces; (5) regenerate facade stubs and OPEN MIGRATION BEADS for every moved symbol; (6) close or classify every new divergence (unclassified BLOCKS the epoch claim); (7) run the unchanged downstream corpus under both epochs; (8) publish the capsule, evidence bundle, and migration report — then move the pin.

Cadence target: a new stable upstream release reaches R3 within four weeks and R4 by the following cycle, lag publicly visible on the dashboard (risk R14: the newest closed epoch stays R4 while later epochs ship R2/R3 previews — latest-parity never implied). Supported-epoch count: initial posture current + previous stable (OQ-10). Epoch bridges (frontier, allowlisted, separately gated) are the pressure-release valve keeping the treadmill from owning the roadmap.

Acceptance: the ratchet runs end-to-end against a SIMULATED epoch advance (a synthetic delta over the current pin) producing the capsule, the census diffs, the auto-opened migration beads, and the block-on-unclassified behavior; the lag dashboard renders from ledger data. Tests: census-diff correctness fixtures (planted deltas per surface must be caught and classified); bead-opening automation tests; block-on-unclassified enforcement test.

title: W1: Substrate & Contracts — fln-core, extraction law, Tribunal bootstrap, baselines
description:
Workstream W1 "Substrate & Contracts" (§22.2): the foundation everything else compiles against, plus the conformance apparatus bootstrapped FIRST so every later workstream is born differential.

Scope: fln-core (names, universe levels, Expr constructor inventory with cached hashes / loose-bvar ranges / has-fvar+has-mvar flags exactly as the Reference exposes them — §1.1; literals, options, byte positions); fln-hash (owned BLAKE3-class hashing, domain-separation registry, canonical serialization schemas); the mechanical contract-extraction toolchain (ABI_CONTRACT.md, OLEAN_CONTRACT.md, extern + builtin censuses per Appendices B/C — extracted from the pin by checked-in scripts, never hand-transcribed, CI re-extracts and fails on drift, Rule D5/D9); the Tribunal rig + differential harness against the Reference binaries (epoch laboratory: hashed official archives, CLI/LSP transcripts, quirk ledgers, per-platform reproduction commands — §18); the W1 baseline performance report of the Reference at the pin on both pinned bare-metal profiles (§19.1 — gate numbers are ratified against it); the dependency-closure allowlist + SUITE.lock ceremony (D1); windows functional CI; G0 coordination.

Depends on nothing; everything depends on it. Exit: contracts generated and CI-locked, Tribunal executes a Reference-vs-Reference smoke differential, baseline report checked in, closure audit green.

title: W7: simp faithful core + deterministic compiled rewrite-index format
description:
Implement the simp faithful core + compiled rewrite indexes (§12.1, §12.2): the Reference simp semantics — simp-set discipline, discrimination-tree candidate retrieval, congruence closure interplay, dsimp/simp-only behaviors, attribute-driven extension — implemented natively end to end (driver, congruence interplay, extension dispatch are Anvil code behind obs-class Mirror facade rows), with the Corpus simp-heavy files plus instrumented-oracle rewrite traces as the fidelity corpus. Mathlib simp EXTENSIONS and simprocs are user code on Golem calling the native driver through the facade.

Compiled rewrite indexes (§12.2): per-library discrimination automata compiled at Ledger commit time and shipped as content-addressed CAS artifacts (an olean-next attachment) — mathlib tens of thousands of simp lemmas become a preloaded matcher, not a per-session index build. Staleness is impossible BY CONSTRUCTION (content-addressed against the simp-set own hash).

Acceptance: simp outcomes match the oracle on the simp-heavy fidelity corpus (rewrite firings, order where observable, result terms); index-backed retrieval produces identical candidate sets to freshly-built trees (representation-equivalence tests); index build determinism (same simp-set hash, same artifact bytes). Tests: golden rewrite-trace replay; dsimp/simp-only behavioral fixtures; congruence-interplay differentials; a stale-index mutation test (perturbed simp-set with unperturbed index must be structurally unloadable).

title: W9: Replay bundles + protocol rigs — deterministic server bugs, editor smoke matrix
description:
Implement session replay bundles + protocol rigs (§14.3, §18.2): every session can emit a replay bundle (init transcript, edit and request sequence, content ids or sealed sources, scheduling profile, normalized outbound transcript); fln server replay bundle reproduces the session and reports the FIRST divergence — server bugs become deterministic objects, not race anecdotes.

Protocol rigs: recorded editor sessions replayed against BOTH servers (FrankenLean and the Reference) with wire-level diffing under registered nondeterminism classes (request interleaving) normalized by versioned normalizer code; the vscode-lean4 smoke matrix in CI (open, edit, hover, goals, completion, go-to-def, widgets, waitForDiagnostics across representative projects incl. a mathlib slice); the C5 corpus grown continuously from real usage.

Acceptance: replay determinism — a bundle replays bit-identically under the lab runtime; the smoke matrix green across the editor scenarios; first-divergence reporting demonstrated on a seeded server mutant. Tests: bundle round-trip fixtures; normalizer-honesty tests (a normalizer may never discard an error body to pass — planted-divergence detection); torture under the asupersync lab: cancellation storms, fault injection, crash-recovery of session state with seed replay (§18.2 torture lane).

title: W8: Cache federation + epoch bridges — validated atp CAS, conservative cross-epoch reuse
description:
Implement cache federation + epoch-stable caching (§13.5, §13.6): the CAS syncs over atp (multi-donor, fountain-coded, resumable) or HTTPS fallback, entries signed and verified; a fln cache shim preserves the lake exe cache muscle memory and hydrates from the community existing archives during transition. Because records are keyed semantically, a cache populated by ANY machine on the epoch serves every project shape — first-mathlib-build becomes: sync frontier, elaborate your delta (PG-10: hydration >= 2x lake exe cache get on the lossy-path fixture, >= 1x clean).

Security law (R18): a remote entry is UNTRUSTED DATA until validated locally — node key and schema, content hashes and closure, epoch/platform compatibility, capability-input binding, kernel/checker receipts where relevant, signature policy, decompression bounds; a malformed entry becomes a miss plus a security event, never trusted-state poison; concurrent fill is lease-suppressed; corruption quarantines are first-class records.

Epoch-stable caching: toolchain bumps re-key the epoch, but the Ledger records WHY — a pin advance ships a machine-diffed census, and records provably untouched by the delta may carry epoch bridges (frontier, off by default, conservative allowlist, gated behind its own soundness rig — OQ-6 owns allowlist v1). The migration autopilot (audit project against the new epoch census, propose rewrites from the repair classes, lockstep builds, apply-transaction with rollback; never edits without explicit apply) is specified here and consumes the Bloodhound repair engine (W11).

Acceptance: PG-10 on the WAN fixtures; validation rejects every corpus of malformed entries; bridge soundness rig green on allowlist v1. Tests: multi-donor pull under injected loss; poisoned-entry fixtures; lease-suppression races under the lab; quarantine-and-revalidate drills.

The complete audit/propose/verify/explicit-apply/rollback workflow is owned by franken_lean-epoch-migration-autopilot-q9w; this bead supplies its bridge identities, validation facts, and rollback-capable cache primitives.

title: W7: Verdict — owned CDCL SAT with owned proof logging and checker
description:
Implement Verdict (fln-verdict, §12.5): an owned CDCL SAT solver — watched literals, VSIDS/CHB-class heuristics, clause management, inprocessing kept modest and honest — with OWNED proof logging and an OWNED independent certificate checker: the bv_decide pipeline bit-blasts through Verdict, Verdict emits a checkable unsatisfiability certificate, and a small independent checker (kernel-adjacent in audit rigor, separate in code) validates it before the reflected proof is admitted. The external-binary TCB (CaDiCaL + LRAT toolchain, weakness census #7) dies here; Behavior Note BN-06 records the change (same theorems, owned certificate chain, no external solver).

Parity posture (risk R17 honesty): correctness is absolute from day one; competitiveness is a ratchet — PG-9 tracks Verdict against the Reference solver on the bv_decide corpus with a published gap budget (100% agreement; wall <= 2x Reference-with-CaDiCaL at G5, ratcheting thereafter). If the gap persists, it PUBLISHES honestly; semantics never depended on solver speed.

Acceptance: 100% verdict agreement on the bv_decide corpus; every unsat certificate validated by the independent checker before admission; PG-9 wall within budget at G5. Tests: SAT/UNSAT differential vs known-good results on standard benchmarks + the bv_decide corpus; certificate-checker independence verified structurally (no shared solver modules); fuzzed CNF + fuzzed bit-blasting inputs under budgets; a planted bogus-certificate must be rejected.

2. E2E coverage: add or extend at least one shared scripts/e2e scenario for the real user-visible path, plus a negative/recovery scenario. Release-level evidence uses the no-mock lane (real Reference binary/artifact/filesystem/editor/process/host as applicable). A mock may support a unit test but may not close an L-level, PG gate, or public claim.
3. Detailed logs: emit both concise human logs and schema-versioned NDJSON with run/scenario/step ids; bead, claim, invariant, Parity-Ledger row, and gate ids; exact command/argv with secrets redacted; cwd; epoch/mode/profile/platform/thread-count/seed/cache-state/host facts; canonical input hashes; expected vs actual; monotonic start/end/duration; resource and budget facts; exit status/signal; stdout/stderr artifact references; receipt/logical roots; first-divergence data; cleanup and asserted final state. Never swallow stderr, never leak secrets, and bound adversarial log volume.
4. CI/closeout: archive logs, fixtures, repro bundles, and evidence manifests; failures must be diagnosable from artifacts alone. Run scripts/check.sh, cargo test, the bead-specific unit/property suites, relevant Tribunal differential/mutation/fault lanes, and the shared E2E scenario. title: Gate G6: Sovereign & Beyond — no-cc executables, Ledger GA, R4 declared
description:
Convergence gate G6 "Sovereign & Beyond" (§22.3): Iron builds real executables with no C compiler; Ledger GA with cache federation and PG-3 (the cone) + PG-10 (cache hydration); the leapfrog surfaces shipped (Bloodhound, Folio, Envoy, terminal InfoView, WASM Judge); W12 distribution complete; --reproducible certified across the matrix. The toolchain is one static binary set that installs in seconds and owes the outside world nothing.

Also at this horizon per the gate table: PG-2 tightens to <= 0.35x Reference; PG-M to <= 0.9x (the native facade beating hosted dispatch — the design bet, measured); native_decide Tier 2 (trace-guided kernel replay) becomes the certified-path default (§11.6).

Evidence bundle: a cold-machine, air-gapped install building and checking a real project end to end (C9); Iron-built executables from real Lake projects with zero cc present; PG-3/PG-10 measurements on the WAN fixtures; the reproducible dual-build byte-identity results across the certified matrix; the R4 declaration for the pinned epoch — every mandatory Parity Ledger row at L4, no fallback code, the Witness matrix consistent with every published claim.

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. title: W3: fln-bignum — owned kernel-grade bignum, ABI-layout limbs, PG-K gate
description:
Implement fln-bignum (§8.4): kernel-grade arbitrary precision replacing GMP — limb-based magnitude representation matching the ABI bignum-object layout (Marrow mpz-shim is a VIEW, not a conversion), schoolbook/Karatsuba/Toom multiplication with thresholds tuned on the KERNEL-REDUCTION corpus (not synthetic benchmarks), Knuth-D division, binary/Lehmer gcd, exact decimal parsing/printing for literals. Constant-time-ness explicitly NOT claimed (this is a prover, not a KMS).

Why owned: GMP sits under every literal in the Reference and inside kernel reduction itself (§1.10) — an unauditable C dependency in the most trusted position. The kernel accelerated Nat operation set (add/sub/mul/div/mod/pow/gcd/comparisons/bitwise per the pin) routes through fln-bignum so decide-class proofs keep the same asymptotics. The crate stays in-repo rather than the suite (kernel-adjacent code answers to the kernel covenant; revisit trigger recorded if a second suite member needs bigints — §2.11).

Acceptance: PG-K gate — kernel-reduction operation mix <= 1.15x the GMP-backed Reference kernel on the arithmetic-heavy fixture set (parity on the small operands that dominate; bounded, PUBLISHED gap on giants); ABI-layout view verified against C4 probes. Tests: differential vectors vs known-good results across the full operation set incl. boundary limb counts; parsing/printing round-trips vs Reference outputs; threshold-tuning harness checked in with the corpus profile it tuned against.

title: W12: Distribution & Epochs — releases, elan layout, epoch ratchet, Witness matrix
description:
Workstream W12 "Distribution & Epochs" (§22.5, §13.6, §17.1): shipping the sovereign toolchain and keeping it current against a monthly upstream without ever lying about parity.

Scope: static binaries per platform (lean, leanc, lake, fln) with the elan-compatible release layout so a lean-toolchain line can name a FrankenLean toolchain and everything downstream just works; the install script; version-line parse compatibility with a machine-readable implementation field + fln identity --json (compatibility never becomes deception, §17.1); reproducible releases (built twice in isolated builders and byte-compared; --reproducible certified matrix; windows bit-certification as a separate declared decision, D17); the epoch-advance ratchet as tooling (the mechanical 8 steps of §22.5: fetch+hash, regenerate censuses on every platform, run corpus differentials, machine-diff all public surfaces, regenerate facade stubs + open migration beads per moved symbol, close-or-classify every divergence — unclassified BLOCKS the epoch claim, run unchanged downstream corpus under both epochs, publish capsule + evidence bundle + migration report); side-by-side epoch installs; the public lag dashboard (target: new stable reaches R3 in four weeks, R4 next cycle); the Witness claim-to-proof matrix + documentation CI (§20.4: docs wording may never exceed evidence state; a benchmark is OBSERVED, never PROVEN).

## Success Criteria

All scope and outcomes stated above remain mandatory, and every dependency/child deliverable required for this epic or gate must be completed at its honest evidence level. The MANDATORY EPIC/GATE CLOSEOUT CONTRACT in Notes must pass with coverage mapping, no-mock E2E evidence, detailed archived human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, reproducible environment facts, and every skip/divergence classified. No feature, compatibility surface, invariant, performance gate, or release claim may be silently narrowed, dropped, or replaced by a prototype.
notes:
MANDATORY EPIC/GATE CLOSEOUT CONTRACT:
This epic/gate may close only when every child deliverable is complete; the verification registry maps every implemented public behavior, FL-INV claim, Parity-Ledger row, Behavior Note, and PG/gate claim to named unit/property/mutation coverage plus a shared no-mock E2E scenario; detailed human + schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts are archived; all divergences and skips are classified; no mock-only evidence is used for a release claim; and the final evidence bundle records exact reproduction commands, environment/epoch/mode/platform/thread/seed/cache facts, input hashes, receipts/logical roots, expected final states, first divergences, and cleanup results.

title: W1: fln-core — names, levels, Expr inventory, options, positions
description:
Implement fln-core (§21, §1.1): the shared vocabulary every crate compiles against. Names (hierarchical, with macro-scope decoration hooks for Quill), universe levels (zero/succ/max/imax/param/mvar with the normalization laws incl. imax u 0 = 0 — Prop impredicativity depends on it), the kernel expression inventory (bvar de Bruijn, fvar, mvar, sort, const+levels, app, lam, forallE, letE, lit Nat/String, mdata, proj) with the Reference-observable accessories: cached hashes, loose-bvar ranges, has-fvar/has-mvar flags (metaprograms and fast traversal pruning read these — they are API, not internals). Options model with the canonical resource limits (maxHeartbeats 200000 in thousand-units, maxRecDepth 512) and the newer resource-limit surface. Byte positions in UTF-8 exactly as upstream computes them (wire-visible through diagnostics and LSP).

Distinct newtypes per semantic kind from day one (DeclNameId, ExprNodeId, BinderDepth, ReductionFuel, EnvCommitId — §8.2b): no integer is accepted as two semantic kinds anywhere in the workspace.

Acceptance: constructor inventory + flag semantics verified against C0 micro-fixtures derived from the pin; hashing observables match the Reference on the fixture corpus. Tests: property tests for level normalization laws; flag-computation unit tests; fixture diff harness wired into fln-conformance.

comments.author: RubyForest
comments.text: Read-only acceptance review findings (2026-07-22), already sent on Agent Mail thread franken_lean-p8a: (1) `scripts/e2e/core_observables.sh` seeds `corrupted.txt` but only diffs it against the pristine fixture; it does not drive the corrupted candidate through the production fixture validator or harness, and an intervening generator call rewrites the live pristine fixture. Parameterize/isolate the check target and prove rejection, recovery, and live-tree non-mutation. (2) `Name.num`, `DataValue.ofNat`/`ofInt`, and `Expr.proj.idx` are unbounded Lean Nat/Int surfaces, but current u64/i64/saturated representations conflate or truncate valid values; the 2^64 fixture checks only the deliberately-colliding overflow hash and masks equality/order/display/round-trip loss. Core needs a durable unbounded value representation even if arithmetic remains fln-bignum work. (3) `core_observables.rs` allocates `seen` but never asserts uniqueness or an exact expected-key set; `records >= 60` permits case deletion plus duplication/substitution, so the claimed closed corpus is not total. Current targeted tests are green but do not cover these gaps.

title: W1: fln-hash — owned hashing, domain separation, canonical serialization
description:
Implement fln-hash (§21): owned BLAKE3-class hashing with a domain-separation registry and canonical serialization schemas — the identity layer beneath the Ledger (decl hashes, logical roots), Grimoire (semantic hash vs byte hash, §7.3), receipts, the transparency log, and every cache key in the program.

Requirements: (a) domain separation is a REGISTRY, not a convention — every hash use site declares its domain tag and CI greps for unregistered hashing; (b) canonical serialization schemas are versioned, with the schema registry shared with the codecs (Appendix B: every durable format specified once, generated into both the codecs and the conformance corpus); (c) the semantic-hash / byte-hash distinction is structural: re-encoding and compression can change bytes without pretending to change meaning; (d) logical roots (§7.1) digest declarations + extension deltas + options while EXCLUDING wall-clock, paths, and scheduler traces — two hosts producing the same trusted environment share a logical root.

Acceptance: hash vectors checked in and stable across platforms; nothing in the program hashes outside this crate (CI-enforced). Tests: unit tests per hash domain with frozen vectors (cross-platform identity on the certified matrix); canonical-serialization round-trip property tests over generated values; a domain-confusion mutation test (same bytes hashed under two domains MUST differ, and a planted unregistered hash call must fail CI); logical-root determinism e2e test — two elaboration runs on different thread counts and (in CI) different hosts produce identical roots, with the full input closure logged in the test output for diagnosis on mismatch.

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. title: W1: Quality gates — scripts/check.sh, CI pipeline, e2e smoke harness with detailed logging
description:
Build the quality-gate infrastructure AGENTS.md promises: scripts/check.sh running cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, and cargo test in order, stopping on first failure — wired as the CI test step (never duplicated inline in workflow files), plus ubs integration on changed files and the e2e smoke harness.

The e2e smoke harness is the always-runnable end-to-end script family with GREAT, DETAILED LOGGING: each script logs every step (command, inputs, expected vs actual, timing, environment fingerprint) to both human-readable and NDJSON streams so failures are diagnosable from CI artifacts alone without reproduction. Initial smokes: workspace-builds-cold (clean checkout -> cargo build), contracts-regenerate (extraction scripts run, drift check), tribunal-smoke (Reference-vs-Reference differential returns clean), and — growing with each gate — judge-smoke (fln check-olean on a small fixture set), parity-smoke (a tiny corpus through T2). Every future subsystem bead extends this family rather than inventing parallel harnesses; test logging conventions (structured events, stable ids, no swallowed stderr) are established here once.

Acceptance: scripts/check.sh green locally and in CI; a seeded failure in each stage stops the pipeline with the failing stage named; e2e logs validate against the NDJSON schema and are archived as CI artifacts. Tests: self-test mode for the harness (planted failures produce correct exit codes and log records); log-schema contract test.

## Acceptance Criteria

All bead-specific requirements, acceptance conditions, tests, invariants, compatibility surfaces, evidence levels, performance gates, negative/recovery cases, and user-visible behaviors stated above remain mandatory. In addition, the MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE in Notes must be satisfied with named unit/property/mutation suites, at least one shared real-path no-mock E2E scenario plus failure/recovery coverage, detailed human and schema-versioned NDJSON artifacts, and reproducible close evidence. No listed feature or claim may be silently narrowed, deferred, mocked, or declared complete through an unclassified divergence.
design:
REVIEW AMENDMENT — this bead is the mandatory verification contract and scenario registry, not only a convenience script.

In addition to the existing scope, define a machine-checked verification manifest schema keyed by bead id, requirement/claim id, FL-INV id, Parity-Ledger row, Behavior Note, workstream, and convergence/performance gate. Every implementation bead must register: deterministic unit tests for success/boundary/error/resource/cancel/failure-atomicity behavior; applicable property, metamorphic, fuzz, mutation-kill, and fault-drill coverage; at least one real user-path E2E scenario and one negative/recovery scenario; and the evidence artifacts that close the requirement. CI fails on an implemented row with no registered coverage, an orphan scenario, a release claim backed only by mocks, an unclassified skip, or a scenario that does not assert its final state.

The shared E2E runner must produce paired human and schema-versioned NDJSON logs containing run/scenario/step correlation ids, bead/claim/invariant/row/gate ids, redacted command argv, cwd, toolchain epoch, mode/profile, platform, thread count, seed, cache state, host facts, canonical input hashes, expected/actual values, monotonic timing, resource/budget facts, exit/signal, stdout/stderr artifact references, receipt/logical roots, first-divergence records, and cleanup/final-state assertions. Logs are bounded and secret-redacted, stderr is never swallowed, and CI archives everything needed to diagnose a failure without rerunning it.

REVIEW AMENDMENT — process supervision and evidence publication are part of the trusted test-result boundary. Every launched stage must be causally contained before target exec: establish the guardian/subreaper, session/process-group identity, start ticks, and pidfd handles behind a bounded child gate, publish readiness only after those bindings exist, then release exec. Never signal a raw numeric PID after an asynchronous gap. Setup/readiness has its own bounded timeout; execution timeout starts only after child-authored readiness. Fast leader exit, detached descendants, PID churn/reuse, pidfd exhaustion, cancellation storms, and every supported signal are mandatory typed cases; successful, semantic-nonzero, unexpected-nonzero, and signalled immediate-exit commands run as a repeated stress matrix with zero false internal faults and zero survivors.

Finalization installs one common HUP/INT/TERM handler atomically before doing work and uses a durable write-once decision protocol. `validate-bundle` remains read-only; a distinct `adopt-bundle` operation may recover a valid decision by O_EXCL marker publication, fsync of marker plus every parent through the artifact root, and full post-adoption revalidation. Relative/absolute artifact arguments are normalized and containment-checked once; parent walks detect fixed points and every finalizer has a bounded termination budget. Concurrent adopters are idempotent, cancellation cannot be adopted as pass, mismatched decision/marker fails closed, and crash points from decision through directory fsync have named recovery tests. Quality runs use a per-attempt isolated Cargo target (or prove cache identity cryptographically), bind UBS inventory to the final governed source, and never promote inconclusive/internal-fault evidence.

REVIEW AMENDMENT — the evidence envelope begins before fallible setup. Once a fresh artifact directory exists, UBS inventory, vendor binding, governed-root hashing, host/argv fact capture, initial event publication, snapshot construction, and every other setup step execute under the same finalization state machine as ordinary stages. Setup failure, concurrent governed-source drift, cancellation, or internal fault must still yield exactly one typed terminal record and a durable independently validatable bundle; the pre-envelope may do only argument validation and atomic creation/refusal of the artifact directory. No run may strand an ambiguous partial directory containing setup artifacts but no verdict.

REVIEW AMENDMENT — authoritative builds run inside a sealed compiler environment. Resolve the exact SUITE.lock toolchain before repository-controlled compilation, invoke cargo and rustc through that explicit rustup channel, and exclude or fail closed on ambient RUSTFLAGS, CARGO_ENCODED_RUSTFLAGS, RUSTC and Cargo rustc-wrapper overrides, RUSTUP_TOOLCHAIN, target-specific rustflags, Cargo-home configuration, and ancestor .cargo configuration. Use per-attempt isolated Cargo home and target directories without deleting or mutating user caches, and record the admitted environment plus compiler path/version/commit in the initial evidence envelope. This is required because pinned rustc accepts deny(unsafe_code) and forbid(unsafe_code) crates containing unsafe when cap-lints allow is injected; source posture and environment isolation are independent defenses.
acceptance_criteria:
The verification manifest and log schemas are frozen with contract tests; every existing task bead has a registered verification obligation; seeded omissions (missing unit coverage, mock-only gate evidence, missing negative E2E, unclassified skip, swallowed stderr, absent final-state assertion, secret leakage, or malformed NDJSON) fail CI with the exact bead and requirement named. scripts/check.sh and the shared runner work locally and in CI, and a planted E2E failure is diagnosable from archived artifacts alone.

Process-boundary acceptance is additive: the built-in self-test and an external 100-iteration immediate-exit matrix are green for exit 0, registered semantic nonzero, unexpected nonzero, and signal termination; delayed child readiness cannot consume execution budget; detached-child and PID-reuse churn leave zero survivors and never signal an unrelated process. Deterministic HUP/INT/TERM injection at spawn, running, terminal-publication, finalizer-entry, decision-written, marker-linked, and post-fsync boundaries always yields one schema-valid terminal record and one durable bundle whose typed verdict matches the linearization point. Relative and absolute publication paths terminate identically; escaping paths fail boundedly; validate is side-effect-free; adoption is durable and concurrently idempotent. One stable-source full scripts/check.sh run and both real no-mock structural E2Es must independently validate their committed bundles before this bead closes.

Setup-boundary acceptance is additive: plant failure and cancellation at every step from artifact-directory creation through the first stage readiness, plus a concurrent governed-file mutation during initial hashing. Every case terminates within budget with one schema-valid terminal event, an explicit setup/inconclusive/internal classification, bounded retained stdout/stderr, and a committed bundle whose manifest inventories the partial setup artifacts. Reused or escaping artifact paths fail before mutation; every fresh accepted path is either durably finalized or carries a separately machine-readable pre-creation refusal outside that path.

Compiler-environment acceptance is additive: a hostile-environment matrix injects cap-lints allow through ordinary and encoded rustflags, fake rustc and workspace wrappers, an alternate toolchain, target-specific rustflags, repository/ancestor/Cargo-home config, and equivalent supported Cargo channels. Every attempt is either rejected before repository compilation with a typed bundle or demonstrably excluded from the effective rustc argv; no hostile marker executes. Each mutation is followed by a clean recovery run, and the bundle independently proves the exact compiler commit and isolated Cargo home/target identity.
notes:
MANDATORY VERIFICATION DEFINITION OF DONE (inherits franken_lean-rur; additive to this bead's own Tests/Acceptance):
1. Unit coverage: deterministic happy-path, boundary, malformed-input, resource-exhaustion, cancellation, and failure-atomicity cases for every new public behavior; property/metamorphic and mutation-kill tests wherever an invariant or equivalence is claimed.
2. E2E coverage: add or extend at least one shared scripts/e2e scenario for the real user-visible path, plus a negative/recovery scenario. Release-level evidence uses the no-mock lane (real Reference binary/artifact/filesystem/editor/process/host as applicable). A mock may support a unit test but may not close an L-level, PG gate, or public claim.
3. Detailed logs: emit both concise human logs and schema-versioned NDJSON with run/scenario/step ids; bead, claim, invariant, Parity-Ledger row, and gate ids; exact command/argv with secrets redacted; cwd; epoch/mode/profile/platform/thread-count/seed/cache-state/host facts; canonical input hashes; expected vs actual; monotonic start/end/duration; resource and budget facts; exit status/signal; stdout/stderr artifact references; receipt/logical roots; first-divergence data; cleanup and asserted final state. Never swallow stderr, never leak secrets, and bound adversarial log volume.
4. CI/closeout: archive logs, fixtures, repro bundles, and evidence manifests; failures must be diagnosable from artifacts alone. Run scripts/check.sh, cargo test, the bead-specific unit/property suites, relevant Tribunal differential/mutation/fault lanes, and the shared E2E scenario. The close evidence names every test/scenario id and artifact location; skips require a typed, logged, honest L-level limitation and may not satisfy the skipped claim.

comments.author: CopperHare

The fail-closed hash check is correct in isolation, but the runner currently cannot produce close evidence during normal concurrent development. comments.author: CopperHare

comments.author: CopperHare
comments.text: Live gate regression after the current signal-handling edits (2026-07-22 10:46 EDT): ./scripts/check.sh exits 2 at evidence-self-test. In target/check/check-20260722T144655Z-1841227/evidence-self-test/semantic_failure/stage.meta.json, a planted child exit 7 is misclassified as cancelled/signal_SIGTERM (wrapper 4, child_exit 7), so self-test aborts with `evidence: semantic exit was not a failure`. The outer check evidence bundle itself commits and validates as typed internal_fault. Please preserve/restore supervisor signal state between self-test cases and add an order-sensitive regression proving a preceding cancellation/terminal-publication test cannot leak SIGTERM state into the semantic-failure case. fln-8mj E2E is green at target/e2e/structure-gate-20260722T143809Z-1629724, but fln-8mj cannot close until this mandatory full gate passes.

comments.author: RubyForest
comments.text: Additional independent failure in the same check run: outer evidence-self-test.meta.json reports supervisor failure EvidenceError: cannot inspect process 1844927: [Errno 3] No such process from /proc/1844927/stat. Current proc_stat_facts treats FileNotFoundError as an ordinary vanished process but routes ProcessLookupError/ESRCH through generic OSError into internal_fault. /proc process lifetime races can surface ESRCH too; classify that as vanished/None and add a rapid-exit race regression. This is distinct from the leaked SIGTERM classification in comment 22.

comments.author: RubyForest
comments.text: Causal correction after correlating metadata/timestamps: the ESRCH race and semantic_failure SIGTERM are probably not independent. The outer supervisor wraps evidence.py self-test; its metadata has the /proc ESRCH error plus resource.term_sent=true. That outer failure occurred while the inner semantic_failure case ran (inner 14:47:05.072-05.152 UTC; outer ended 05.244), so terminate_tree legitimately delivered SIGTERM to the self-test process and the inner case observed it. There is not yet evidence of a prior-case signal-state leak. First make proc_stat_facts treat ESRCH as an ordinary vanished process, then rerun the order-sensitive signal test to determine whether a separate restoration bug remains.

comments.author: CopperHare
comments.text: Follow-up live supervisor race after the 18-case self-test passed (2026-07-22 10:52 EDT): ./scripts/check.sh now passes evidence-self-test, shellcheck, fmt, check, and clippy, but exits 2 during cargo test. target/check/check-20260722T145218Z-1952497/test.meta.json records `supervisor failure: EvidenceError: cannot inspect process 1959206: [Errno 3] No such process`; child_exit is null and the supervisor sent TERM even though the direct fresh-target full cargo test is green. This is a normal short-lived cargo test subprocess race in descendant discovery. proc_children/live_tree_members must treat ENOENT after task enumeration as process exit, not internal fault, while still failing closed on permission/malformed-proc errors. Add a high-churn many-short-children stress case (repeat enough to hit PID/task disappearance between enumeration/read/open) and prove pass classification plus zero survivors.

comments.author: RubyForest
comments.text: Fresh static blocker in the active check.sh finalizer edit: it now accepts any existing validate-bundle-green marker even when complete-bundle returned nonzero, claiming validation durably adopts it. validate-bundle is read-only; it does not prove the marker link and parent-directory entry were fsynced. A crash or SIGKILL after link but before directory fsync can leave a valid-looking yet non-durable marker, and an actual fsync error is also being rounded up to success. Preserve fail-closed semantics unless a typed adoption operation explicitly fsyncs the marker and parent directory, then revalidates all bindings; distinguish post-commit signal from publication/fsync failure. Mere existence plus content validation is not durability evidence.

comments.author: RubyForest
comments.text: New deterministic-test blocker from target/check/check-20260722T150547Z-2286209: evidence-self-test exited 2 after the timeout case was correctly classified, because timeout-pids.txt did not yet exist. The 250 ms timeout starts before spawn/scheduling and the test immediately reads the child-created file after supervision, so machine load can terminate the child before it publishes descendant identities. Do not just weaken the survivor assertion. Add an explicit child-ready handshake/test hook before starting the timeout clock (or run the wrapper asynchronously, wait for the PID file/readiness, then arm a deterministic timeout), and retain proof that every published PID is dead. This is a real order/load flake in a mandatory gate.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Fresh umbrella-gate finding (2026-07-22 11:06 EDT): check run target/check/check-20260722T150547Z-2286209 failed evidence self-test before cargo gates because self_test reads timeout-pids.txt unconditionally after a 250 ms supervised timeout, but under host load the child had not executed the write before timeout/termination (Errno 2). The timeout case currently tests scheduling luck, not just descendant cleanup. Please make setup deterministic (for example, have the child publish a readiness sentinel/PID file and have the test wait with a bounded monotonic deadline before arming the timeout, or otherwise separate setup from the timed operation); preserve typed inconclusive semantics and add a delayed-start regression. Bundle correctly committed as internal_fault, so this is a harness self-test defect, not fln-8mj product failure.

comments.author: ubuntu

During the same run, the governed UBS inventory changed and finalization correctly refused close evidence. comments.author: RubyForest
comments.text: Static recheck after the latest 19-case self-test happened to pass: the timeout race from comments 31/32 is still present in current scripts/evidence.py. The timeout case starts a supervised 250 ms deadline and then unconditionally reads timeout-pids.txt; stage.ready.json only proves the supervisor spawned the child, not that the child executed its PID-file write. There is no child-ready handshake before the timeout clock. A loaded host can still kill the child before line 3870 and reproduce Errno 2. Please do not treat one green self-test as remediation; add a child-created readiness sentinel and arm the timed phase only after a bounded wait, while retaining zero-survivor proof. Also, target/check/check-20260722T150920Z-2390203 began during the 15:09:59 evidence.py edit and ended missing supervisor metadata/incomplete bundle, so it is not close evidence.

comments.author: RubyForest
comments.text: New deterministic CLI-supervisor blocker against current scripts/evidence.py: `python3 scripts/evidence.py run ... -- /usr/bin/true` returns 2/internal_fault instead of pass. Artifact target/check/rur-cli-run-parser-probe-20260722T1517Z/stage.meta.json reports `supervisor failure: EvidenceError: cannot bind child process lifetime`; readiness is incorrectly labeled spawn_failed even though exec succeeded. Race: run_supervised reads non-zombie child facts, then remember_process/pidfd binding loses the very short-lived child and promotes a successful stage to internal fault. Add real CLI-parser/supervisor smoke cases for immediate exit 0 and immediate semantic/nonsemantic nonzero exits. The fix must bind/control the child lifetime before it can execute/exit (or otherwise prove terminal status and descendant scope safely); do not merely ignore failed pidfd binding, since a fast leader could leave detached descendants.

comments.author: RubyForest

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Design note for comments 37/41 (no code edit; PinkCat still owns the path): a race-free supervisor needs an exec barrier, not a retry around pidfd_open. One viable Linux design is an internal child-gate executable/subcommand that becomes the session leader, self-stops before exec, and later execs the requested argv in the same PID after the parent binds start-ticks + pidfd + pgid and publishes readiness; the parent then SIGCONT via pidfd. Give the gate its own bounded setup-failure protocol and keep argv logging bound to the actual target, not the wrapper. Add immediate exit {0, registered semantic nonzero, unregistered nonzero, signal} stress tests plus a gate-that-fails-before-exec case and a target-that-detaches-a-child-then-exits case. For the timeout test, separately wait on a child-authored readiness sentinel under a bounded setup deadline, then arm the 250 ms execution budget; supervisor-spawn readiness is insufficient.

comments.author: RubyForest
comments.text: Source-stability confirmation for comment 38: a second 20-run `/usr/bin/true` CLI-supervisor stress on an unchanged scripts/evidence.py inode mtime (1784734114 before and after) produced 6/20 false internal_fault outcomes (iterations 2,11,12,15,17,19). Artifacts target/check/rur-immediate-exit-stress2-20260722T1530Z-{1..20}. This removes the possible active-edit confound from the first stress result.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Review of the new bundle.decision recovery protocol (current evidence.py, 2026-07-22 11:37 EDT): validate_bundle now creates/links bundle.complete.json and fsyncs the bundle when only bundle.decision exists. That makes a command named validate-bundle state-mutating and turns ordinary read-only inspection into the recovery/commit linearization path. Please split this into an explicit typed adopt-bundle operation (decision validation -> canonical marker O_EXCL link -> marker/parent/ancestor fsync -> full revalidation) and keep validate-bundle strictly read-only; have shell finalizers invoke adopt only when complete-bundle failed but a valid decision exists, then invoke validate. Add tests proving validate alone never creates files, adoption is idempotent under concurrent adopters, a cancellation sentinel cannot be adopted, mismatched decision/marker fails, and crash after decision/before marker recovers durably. Direct current self-test passes 21 cases at target/evidence-self-test/copperhare-decision-probe-20260722t1137, but it does not cover this API side-effect contract or resolve the fast-exit/timeout races.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Static signal-safety blocker in current shell revision: ACTIVE_RUNNER_START_TICKS is populated in check.sh/structure_gate.sh/closure_audit.sh, but never read. stop_active_runner still begins with raw `kill -s "$name" "$pid"` and all liveness checks are raw /proc PID reads; a wrapper can exit and its PID be reused between capture/signal, especially in the SPAWNING pending-signal path. The new variable therefore proves nothing. Bind the wrapper lifetime before clearing SPAWNING and route TERM/liveness through a pidfd/start-tick-checked helper; on inability to bind, do not signal a raw PID. Add a deterministic fast-wrapper-exit plus injected pending-signal test that churns PIDs and proves no unrelated process is signaled. This is separate from the inner supervisor child-exec barrier in comments 37/41/42.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: New loaded-host self-test flake in PinkCat run target/evidence-self-test/postfix-review-final-20260722T1141Z: the first large_output_pass case was typed inconclusive/timeout (5 s budget, 10.49 s total including termination), with only ~0.30 CPU seconds consumed and both bounded tails correctly retained. The child was scheduler-starved/pipe-blocked long enough that the nominal happy-path timeout, not the behavior under test, failed. Keep deliberately short timing only in timeout/cancellation cases; give ordinary correctness cases a generous setup/execution budget or a deterministic progress handshake so shared-host contention cannot turn them into false harness failures. Preserve inconclusive classification (do not retry/promote it inside evidence); the outer gate may rerun a fresh attempt, but only a clean pass may close.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Authoritative-gate isolation finding (check artifact target/check/check-20260722T154516Z-3101780): default-target clippy failed with unresolved fln_core::{expr,lean_hash,level,name,options} even though current crates/fln-core/src/lib.rs exports all five and the immediately preceding cargo check passed. A fresh isolated target (`CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/cargo-target-fln-clippy-proof-20260722t1146 cargo clippy --locked --all-targets -- -D warnings`) compiled every crate and exited 0 in 3.3 s. This is stale/shared Cargo target contamination (also previously observed as 26 vs current 29 structure-guard unit tests), not a source failure. scripts/check.sh must allocate a per-run target directory or cryptographically bind/validate any reused RCH target to the exact governed root/toolchain/profile; record it in evidence. Add a regression that seeds a stale same-package rmeta/test binary and proves the gate executes the current source. Never cargo clean/delete shared caches; use isolated targets.

comments.author: RubyForest
comments.text: Deterministic CPU-spin blocker found from the live nested-binding probes (2026-07-22 11:55 EDT). `complete_bundle` passes caller-supplied `manifest_path`/`digest_path` into `durably_sync_manifested_bundle`; that helper absolutizes `art_dir` but appends those two paths to `files` without absolutizing/require_within normalization. For a valid CLI invocation using relative paths, `parent = path.parent` remains relative while `art_dir` is absolute, so `while parent != art_dir` can never terminate; once `parent` reaches `.`, `parent.parent == parent` and it spins forever at 100% CPU. Live counterexamples are the relative-path complete-bundle processes for target/evidence-self-test/nested-binding-audit-20260722T155227Z. Normalize and containment-check manifest/digest/commit paths once at the helper boundary, and make the parent walk explicitly fail if it reaches a fixed point before art_dir. Add relative and absolute CLI regression cases plus an escaping-path case; all must terminate under a bounded outer harness with identical semantics.

comments.author: CopperHare
comments.text: New deterministic nested-finalization failure in PinkCat probe target/check/manual-20260722T-nested-signal-v2 (2026-07-22 11:53 EDT): the inner planted `false` stage was recorded classification=fail/reason=child_exit_semantic_failure, then validate-run rejected it as `planted failure contract is inconsistent`. The outer wrapper correctly reached internal_fault/process_tree_cleanup_unproven, but its `evidence.py complete-bundle` process 3362819 has remained CPU-bound (~92% CPU, >3 minutes) instead of finishing/failing within a bounded publication budget; parent check/self-test and driver remain waiting. This is both a self-test contract mismatch and unbounded finalization defect. Add a unit case for planted semantic failure metadata/validation agreement and a complete-bundle termination budget or demonstrably finite algorithm; retain the artifact and do not promote this probe to close evidence.

comments.author: RubyForest
comments.text: Post-fix source-stable regression (2026-07-22 12:05 EDT): 20 real CLI `evidence.py run ... -- /usr/bin/true` invocations against unchanged evidence.py identity 1784736052:201055 produced 3/20 false internal_fault results (iterations 1, 13, 19). All three metadata files report `supervisor_or_capture_failure` / `EvidenceError: cannot bind child process lifetime`, null child exit, and zero survivors. Artifact: target/check/rur-immediate-exit-postfix-20260722T160506Z. comments.author: RubyForest
comments.text: Verified finalizer-entry signal hole in all three current shells. `on_exit` installs HUP, INT, and TERM handlers in three separate `trap` commands (check.sh:371-373; closure_audit.sh:255-257; structure_gate.sh:255-257). A different signal arriving between those commands still invokes the old `on_signal`, whose `exit` aborts the already-running EXIT trap before terminal/manifest/bundle publication. Minimal Bash reproduction exited 143 with exactly `exit-enter` then `old-signal` and never `exit-finished`. Install one common HUP/INT/TERM finalizer handler atomically in a single `trap ... HUP INT TERM` command before any finalization work, then decode the signal inside it (or use one mode-aware handler throughout). Add deterministic entry-window HUP/INT/TERM tests proving a typed, committed final bundle; a green ordinary full gate does not cover this race.

Artifacts: target/check/rur-immediate-exit-current-20260722T161301Z-3671216. The immediately preceding source-stable built-in self-test passed 21 cases at target/evidence-self-test/rur-current-20260722T161205Z-3653372, demonstrating that current self-test coverage still misses the ordinary fast-exit lifetime-binding race. RUR/fln-8mj must remain open until child exec/lifetime identity is causally bound and a repeated immediate-exit matrix is deterministically green.

SHA-256 was 633f998950307129a8ff186f15d9b314f1320b96c7a9935b31d2ef004939be4 before and after. Artifacts: target/check/rur-immediate-exit-latest-20260722T162102Z-3816809.
